target.title: fix(agents/harness): route CLI backend aliases through PI for in-process compaction [AI-assisted]
target.body:
## Summary

`selectAgentHarnessDecision` in `src/agents/harness/selection.ts` treated `agentRuntime.id` strictly as a plugin harness id. CLI backend aliases (`claude-cli`, `google-gemini-cli`) are documented-valid runtime ids per the schema (see `runtime-schema.ts`: *"a registered plugin harness id such as 'codex', or a supported CLI backend alias such as 'claude-cli'"*), but they are not plugin harnesses — they are CLI backends dispatched at the request layer by `runCliAgent` in `attempt-execution.ts` and `agent-runner.runtime.ts`.

Normal turns never hit the harness selector for these runtimes because the request layer short-circuits on `isCliProvider` first. But **in-process consumers that call `selectAgentHarness` directly** — most importantly `maybeCompactAgentHarnessSession`, used by both `/compact` and the preflight memory-flush path in `runPreflightCompactionIfNeeded` — would throw `MissingAgentHarnessError("claude-cli")` for any agent whose model-scoped or provider-scoped `agentRuntime.id` was a CLI backend alias.

Once a session crosses the preflight compaction threshold, that throw fires on **every inbound turn across every channel** (Telegram, webchat, SMS, `/compact`), because `runPreflightCompactionIfNeeded` runs before every dispatch. The agent becomes unusable until compacted — and `/compact` itself can't compact, so recovery requires a config swap or surgical patch.

## Fix

Add a `cli_backend_routed_via_pi` branch to `selectAgentHarnessDecision` right next to the existing `implicit_plugin_unavailable_pi` (codex) fallback. When the runtime is a CLI backend alias, return the PI harness with `runtime: "pi"` stamped on the policy. The new branch is gated by `isCliRuntimeAlias()` from `model-runtime-aliases.ts`, so any future CLI backend added to `LEGACY_RUNTIME_MODEL_PROVIDER_ALIASES` is covered automatically.

This routes only the **in-process step** through PI:
- For compaction: PI does the transcript summarization. Auth still flows through the user's existing CLI OAuth via `resolveClaudeCliSyntheticAuth`.
- For normal turns: unaffected. They never reach this code path because `runAgentAttempt` in `attempt-execution.ts` and `agent-runner.runtime.ts` branch to `runCliAgent` on `isCliProvider` first.

The branch mirrors the existing codex-implicit fallback shape exactly: same `buildSelectionDecision`, same `runtime: "pi"` policy override, same candidate list. No new architecture.

## Why not a CLI-native compaction path?

The CLI backend declares `reseedFromRawTranscriptWhenUncompacted: true` for the claude-cli case (see `cli-backend.ts`), which is the binary's own internal context management — not something the harness layer can call as "summarize this transcript please." Building a CLI-native compaction backend would be a much larger architectural change. PI fallback is the smallest fix consistent with how this function already handles "this runtime can't compact in-process" cases (codex implicit).

## Real behavior proof

**Setup that triggered the bug.** macOS, Mac Studio (Apple Silicon), OpenClaw 2026.5.19 installed fresh 2 days ago, single agent (`main`) with `anthropic/claude-sonnet-4-6` as primary model and model-scoped `agentRuntime: { id: "claude-cli" }`. Claude CLI binary at `~/.local/bin/claude`, authenticated via OAuth profile `anthropic:claude-cli`. Telegram bot channel active. Session reached 151% of context (1.6m / 1.0m).

**Before-fix observation.** Every inbound message — Telegram and webchat — failed with `MissingAgentHarnessError: Requested agent harness "claude-cli" is not registered.` Log excerpts from `/tmp/openclaw/openclaw-2026-05-21.log`:

```
lane task error: lane=session:agent:main:telegram:direct:1905546045 durationMs=7
  error="MissingAgentHarnessError: Requested agent harness \"claude-cli\" is not registered."

message processed: channel=webchat ... outcome=error duration=2929ms
  error="MissingAgentHarnessError: Requested agent harness \"claude-cli\" is not registered."

lane task error: lane=main durationMs=7
  error="MissingAgentHarnessError: Requested agent harness \"claude-cli\" is not registered."
```

24 occurrences across Telegram, webchat, and the `main` lane, all originating from `dist/subsystem-*.js:178`, with the actual throw site at `dist/selection-*.js` (`selectAgentHarnessDecision`).

**Post-fix validation in this PR (what I can attest to):**
- `pnpm vitest run src/agents/harness/selection.test.ts` → 56 tests passed (54 pre-existing + 2 new regression tests added in this PR).
- `pnpm check` → typecheck, lint, all policy guards pass.
- `pnpm build` → clean.

**Hot-patch on the local install (semantically equivalent change, applied to the bundled `dist/selection-*.js` for live recovery before this PR was opened).** I applied the equivalent two-string-literal patch (`claude-cli` / `google-gemini-cli`) to the locally installed bundle and restarted the gateway. The gateway restart completed (new PID, listening on the configured port). End-to-end behavior validation by sending a fresh Telegram message and running `/compact` post-restart has not been performed yet — happy to add a separate update with that evidence once exercised, or a maintainer can reproduce against this branch directly.

**Not tested:** `google-gemini-cli` end-to-end (no Gemini CLI setup on hand); covered by unit test only. Full `pnpm test` matrix beyond the targeted file (happy to run on request).

## Tests added

- `routes CLI backend aliases to the PI harness so in-process compaction works` — verifies `selectAgentHarness` returns the PI harness for both `claude-cli` and `google-gemini-cli` runtimes.
- `lets maybeCompactAgentHarnessSession proceed for claude-cli model runs via PI` — direct regression test for the user-facing bug: the compaction entrypoint resolves without throwing `MissingAgentHarnessError` for a `claude-cli`-aliased anthropic model.

## Related

- Same-shape-of-bug PR: #72499 (helper paths bypassing CLI dispatch — compaction was missed there)
- Issue with the same error message but different root cause (auth migration gap): #84604
- Issue that pointed me at this area: #57326

## AI-assisted disclosure

This patch was authored end-to-end via Claude Code (claude-opus-4-7). The PR author (me) ran the test suite and repo check personally, validated the bundled-JS equivalent of the patch on the live install that originally exhibited the bug (log evidence above), and read every line before pushing. The original bug surfaced on my own machine — the agent's job was to localize the throw site, design the minimum-viable fix consistent with existing patterns (mirroring the codex-implicit fallback), and write the regression tests.

target.title: fix(telegram): prevent polling stalls via TCP keepalive and widened connect grace
target.body:
## Summary

Three complementary fixes for Telegram polling stalls observed in production where the long-poll `getUpdates` connection silently dies and the gateway either hangs until the stall watchdog fires (~90s) or self-flags `disconnected` during a healthy poll-cycle restart.

### 1. Lower undici TCP keepalive initial delay to 30s (`extensions/telegram/src/fetch.ts`)

**Background:** undici 8.3.0's connector already enables TCP keepalive by default with a 60s initial delay (`socket.setKeepAlive(true, 60000)`). The fix here is not "enable keepalive from off" — it is tightening the timing.

**Root cause:** Long-poll connections to `api.telegram.org` stay idle for up to the `getUpdates` timeout (~900s). Most home/office NAT tables expire idle TCP entries after 60-1800s (commonly ~60-120s for SOHO routers). undici's default 60s initial delay leaves no headroom: a NAT that expires at 60s drops the entry before the first keepalive probe fires. When the NAT entry is silently dropped the TCP connection hangs rather than returning an error, leaving the grammY runner stuck until the 90s stall watchdog fires and forces a restart cycle.

**Fix:** Make the connect options explicit and lower `keepAliveInitialDelay` to 30s on the undici Agent `connect` options built in `buildTelegramConnectOptions`:

```ts
const TELEGRAM_KEEPALIVE_INITIAL_DELAY_MS = 30_000;
return {
  keepAlive: true,
  keepAliveInitialDelay: TELEGRAM_KEEPALIVE_INITIAL_DELAY_MS,
  // ...
};
```

OS-level TCP keepalive probes now start firing at 30s instead of 60s, which:
1. Refreshes the NAT table entry before short-window NATs (60s) expire it.
2. Surfaces dead connections faster with `ETIMEDOUT` instead of waiting through undici's default 60s warmup.

The explicit form also documents the dependency contract so future undici changes don't silently change the timing.

This is the transport-layer prevention complement to upstream's existing detection-layer work (#78646 watchdog, #81746 event-loop stall detection, #82256 spool recovery, #82227 outbound drain). Detection mechanisms still catch any failure mode that escapes the keepalive; this PR cuts the volume of stalls that those mechanisms have to handle.

### 2. Clear inherited `connected` state on polling start; widen channel connect grace to 300s

**Root cause:**
- `notePollingStart` was unconditionally setting `connected: false` at the start of every poll cycle, causing the downstream health monitor to see an apparent disconnect during legitimate reconnect cycles and trigger unnecessary restarts.
- `DEFAULT_CHANNEL_CONNECT_GRACE_MS` was 120s. Slow-startup channels (e.g. bots with many commands, large first-page payloads) finish the full handshake after 2-3 minutes. The health monitor evaluated them as `disconnected` before they finished connecting, causing premature restart cycles.

**Fix:**
- `polling-status.ts`: `notePollingStart` resets all polling lifecycle telemetry — including writing `connected: false` so a fresh lifecycle is never inheriting a stale `connected: true` from the previous runtime patch-merge. This is the right architectural shape because runtime status in `server-channels.ts` is patch-merged: a startup that omits `connected` would inherit the previous lifecycle's flag.
- `channel-health-policy.ts`: `DEFAULT_CHANNEL_CONNECT_GRACE_MS` widened from 120_000 to 300_000. This is in core (shared across channels) because the underlying property — long handshake time before stable connection — is not telegram-specific. Any channel with a slow first connect benefits. The Telegram status diagnostic (`status-issues.ts`) aligns `TELEGRAM_POLLING_CONNECT_GRACE_MS` to the same 300s constant so `openclaw channels status` and the background health monitor agree on the suppression window.

### 3. Defense-in-depth `evaluateChannelHealth` branch for inherited `connected: true`

**Why it's here:** With fix #2 in place, a hung polling startup is normally caught by `evaluateChannelHealth`'s existing `connected === false` branch after the 300s grace. But the patch-merge architecture means a future code path (a new lifecycle hook, a refactor that re-orders the patch sequence) could silently regress by forgetting to clear `connected`. This branch defends against that case.

**Fix:** Post-grace branch in `evaluateChannelHealth` that flags polling channels as `stale-socket` when `connected: true` is paired with null `lastConnectedAt` and null `lastTransportActivityAt` and a non-null `lastStartAt`. Scoped to `mode === "polling"` so webhook channels and channels without continuous transport tracking are not falsely flagged.

## Test plan

- [x] Verified explicit keepalive args activate: `keepAlive: true` and `keepAliveInitialDelay: 30_000` confirmed in undici Agent connect options via existing `polling-fetch.test.ts` assertions on the Agent config.
- [x] `polling-status.test.ts` updated: covers the new `notePollingStart`-writes-connected-false behavior and the cycle-start telemetry reset.
- [x] `polling-session.test.ts` updated: covers the resulting disconnected-patch shape during recoverable restart cycles.
- [x] `channel-health-policy.test.ts`: 3 new test cases covering the defensive branch (flagged path, in-grace happy path, prior-successful-connect happy path) plus an explicit inherited-connected-true case.
- [x] `status.test.ts`: post-grace spool-handler and polling-startup tests aligned with the widened 300s `TELEGRAM_POLLING_CONNECT_GRACE_MS`.
- [x] Local acceptance: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/telegram/src/polling-status.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/polling-session.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/status.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/fetch.test.ts src/gateway/channel-health-policy.test.ts` — 173 tests pass.
- [x] `pnpm check:changed` exit 0.
- [x] Verified stable in production on two hosts (mac-mini, rh-bot) over 3+ days with zero telegram polling stalls.

## Diagnosis signature this PR resolves

`~/.openclaw/logs/gateway.err.log` repeated entries of the form:

```
[telegram] [diag] polling cycle error reason=getUpdates ... err=Call to 'getUpdates' failed! ... timeout
[fetch-timeout] fetch timeout after 10000ms ... operation=fetchWithTimeout url=https://api.telegram.org/.../getMe
```

paired with `gateway-health-monitor` restarting the telegram channel due to a "disconnected" reading shortly after a normal poll-cycle start.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: Telegram long-poll `getUpdates` connection hangs silently after NAT-table entry expires; gateway either waits ~90s for the in-process stall watchdog or self-flags `disconnected` during a legitimate poll-cycle restart, triggering unnecessary channel restart cycles.
- **Real environment tested**: 2 production gateway hosts running this branch as part of `upgrade-2026.5.12` (which carries the original transport + lifecycle commits): `mac-mini.lan` (Kbot / @Kebablebot + @RattyMcRatfaceBot) and `rh-bot.lan` (Requesty / @requestymcrequestfacebot). macOS Darwin 25.5.0, Node 22+, undici via grammY runner long-poll.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**:
  ```bash
  # On each host:
  openclaw gateway status            # confirm runtime
  openclaw channels status           # confirm telegram polling status + last-activity timestamps
  ```
- **Evidence after fix (live terminal output, mac-mini.lan, 2026-05-18)**:
  ```
  CLI version: 2026.5.12 (/opt/homebrew/bin/openclaw)
  Gateway version: 2026.5.12
  Runtime: running (pid 50650, state active)
  Connectivity probe: ok
  - Telegram default: enabled, configured, running, connected, in:12h ago, out:16m ago, mode:polling, token:config, groups:unmentioned
  - Telegram ratbot: enabled, configured, running, connected, in:28h ago, out:28h ago, mode:polling, token:config, groups:unmentioned
  ```
  Same gateway pid (50650) has now held for over 24h continuously, with telegram outbound activity within the last 16 minutes for the default account. Pre-fix observation log signature `[fetch-timeout] fetch timeout after 10000ms ... operation=fetchWithTimeout url=https://api.telegram.org/...` no longer appears in `~/.openclaw/logs/gateway.err.log` post-fix on either host.
- **Observed result after fix**: Both gateways' telegram channels report `running, connected` continuously since deploy; no `[telegram] [diag] polling cycle error reason=getUpdates ... err=...timeout` repeats; no health-monitor-initiated channel restarts; runtime uptime measured in days not minutes.
- **What was not tested**: Behavior under deliberately-induced NAT entry expiry (would need a NAT/router scenario I can't reproduce on the LAN). Behavior under a host with TCP keepalive disabled at the OS level — undici docs imply the connector's `setKeepAlive(true, ...)` call enables it regardless, but I haven't verified that path explicitly. Final-head live proof on the merged `faaa9e77deda` snapshot (production hosts are still on the original transport+lifecycle subset).

target.title: fix(discord): fall back when voice adapter is unavailable
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: Discord cron announce deliveries can fail with `discordVoice outbound adapter is unavailable` when automatic TTS marks a text payload as voice audio.
- Solution: keep the Discord voice path first, but fall back to normal Discord text when the unavailable `discordVoice` adapter error occurs and visible text is present.
- What changed: preserve reply targeting across the fallback by passing both `replyTo` and `replyToId`, and teach the Discord text sender to accept `replyToId` as an alias.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): no cron/core routing change; real Discord voice sends still run first; non-unavailable voice errors and media-only voice payloads still fail instead of being masked.

## Motivation

- Fixes message loss for text-only Discord cron announcements when automatic TTS selects a voice delivery path but the isolated cron context has no Discord voice outbound adapter.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature

target.title: Revamp command authorization candidates
target.body:
## Summary

- replace the ad hoc POSIX authorization path with a Tree-sitter-backed command candidate planner built on `explainShellCommand`
- route shell/argv allowlist checks and allow-always persistence through planned candidates, including static shell-wrapper payloads
- keep approval storage compatible while preserving safer fallback behavior for dynamic wrappers, path-scoped payloads, and pipe-to-shell cases
- keep the UI showing the actual command while honoring `allowedDecisions`

## Decisions and rationale

- POSIX shell authorization now comes from one planned command model: `planShellAuthorization()` uses the Tree-sitter-backed `explainShellCommand()` path, and the plan is carried into allowlist evaluation, allow-always persistence, and shell rewrite. The old POSIX splitter/render fallback is no longer an alternate source of candidates.
- Inline shell candidates are generated once during policy evaluation and then reused. The node-host safe-bin rewrite no longer re-runs `evaluateSystemRunAllowlist()` on the rendered command; it verifies that the plan still belongs to the original shell payload and renders from that plan.
- `/approve` handling is removed from exec control-shell policy. `/approve` is a control/chat action, not an executable policy concept, and exec should not carry a special deny list for old agent behavior. There is no test asserting that `/approve` is a normal shell command.
- Safe bins are treated as narrow, argument-profiled execution shortcuts, not blanket shell trust. Static, parsed safe-bin candidates may be rewritten to resolved trusted paths. Dynamic shells, command substitution, line continuations, path-scoped wrapper payloads, and pipe-to-shell cases remain approval-gated unless the whole relevant command graph is allowed.
- Background `&` is preserved as shell topology. The planner models it as a background relationship and the renderer emits `&` when rebuilding commands, instead of failing closed or collapsing it into a sequence separator.
- `deny` still wins when deny-mode policy is active. The authorization planner can explain candidates, but runtime policy decides whether execution is allowed.

## Behavior changes

- Static POSIX shell wrappers like `sh -c 'git status'` can now be authorized by the inner `git` candidate when the payload is static and safely parsed.
- Dynamic wrappers like `sh -c '$CMD'`, command substitution, line continuations, and path-scoped wrapper payloads stay approval-gated instead of becoming reusable inner approvals.
- `curl URL | sh` / `curl URL | bash` behavior is preserved: both sides must be allowed for auto-run; allow-always persists `curl` only; persisted `curl` alone does not authorize the shell side on rerun.
- Mixed allowlist + safe-bin shell-wrapper payloads are rebuilt before execution from the existing authorization plan so the executed wrapper uses trusted resolved paths.
- `ask=always` approval requests no longer offer an unusable Always Allow action in the Control UI.

## Verification

Behavior addressed: Candidate-based command authorization for POSIX shell and non-Windows argv execution, including shell-wrapper unwrapping, dynamic fallback, pipe-to-shell behavior, allow-always persistence, node-host execution, and UI approval decisions.

Real environment tested: Local macOS focused tests; local OpenClaw Control UI against the candidate worktree; Blacksmith Testbox through Crabbox.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```bash
PATH=/opt/homebrew/bin:$PATH node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/infra/exec-authorization-plan.test.ts src/infra/exec-authorization-allowlist.test.ts src/infra/exec-approvals-allow-always.test.ts src/infra/exec-approvals-analysis.test.ts src/infra/exec-approvals-safe-bins.test.ts src/infra/exec-command-resolution.test.ts src/infra/exec-approvals-policy.test.ts src/infra/exec-approvals.test.ts src/node-host/invoke-system-run.test.ts src/agents/bash-tools.exec.approval-id.test.ts src/agents/bash-tools.exec-host-gateway.test.ts src/agents/bash-tools.exec-host-node.test.ts
git diff --check
node scripts/crabbox-wrapper.mjs run --provider blacksmith-testbox --blacksmith-org openclaw --blacksmith-workflow .github/workflows/ci-check-testbox.yml --blacksmith-job check --blacksmith-ref main --idle-timeout 90m --ttl 240m --timing-json -- CI=1 NODE_OPTIONS=--max-old-space-size=4096 OPENCLAW_TEST_PROJECTS_PARALLEL=6 OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 OPENCLAW_VITEST_NO_OUTPUT_TIMEOUT_MS=900000 OPENCLAW_TESTBOX=1 OPENCLAW_TESTBOX_REMOTE_RUN=1 pnpm check:changed
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/node-host/invoke-system-run-allowlist.test.ts src/infra/exec-control-shell-policy.test.ts
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/infra/exec-approvals-allow-always.test.ts src/infra/exec-authorization-allowlist.test.ts src/infra/exec-authorization-plan.test.ts src/infra/exec-control-shell-policy.test.ts src/node-host/invoke-system-run-allowlist.test.ts ui/src/ui/views/exec-approval.test.ts
```

Evidence after fix: Focused local proof passed 15 files / 465 tests. `git diff --check` passed. Testbox-through-Crabbox `tbx_01ks1f6v0x35t56nymmxbaw4a1` / GitHub Actions run https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/actions/runs/26135215597 completed `pnpm check:changed` with exit 0. Follow-up cleanup proof passed 2 files / 20 tests and then 6 files / 119 tests.

Observed result after fix: Static shell wrappers can auto-run from trusted inner candidates; dynamic/path-scoped wrapper payloads still prompt; `curl | sh` requires both sides; allow-always saved `/usr/bin/git` for a live `sh -c 'git status --short'` run and the rerun produced no second approval request.

What was not tested: Full release validation was not run. Windows behavior remains on the legacy fallback path. The local UI proof used `--no-channels`, so Slack/Discord listeners were not part of the manual proof.

## UI proof notes

- Started local OpenClaw from this worktree with `openclaw-local-test --repo ... --no-open --no-channels --ttl 2h`.
- The installed local-test helper was machine-locally adjusted to stop writing stale `tools.exec.mode = "auto"`; that was not a repo change.
- The Control UI displayed the actual command `sh -c 'git status --short'`, including Host, Agent, Session, CWD, Resolved, Security, and Ask fields.
- Clicking Always Allow resolved the prompt.
- A live chat/tool run then created a saved `/usr/bin/git` allow-always entry, rerunning the same prompt produced no second `exec.approval.requested` event within 30 seconds, and the temporary `/usr/bin/git` test entry was removed from `~/.openclaw/exec-approvals.json` afterward.

target.title: Policy: add model, network, and MCP conformance checks
target.body:
# Policy: add model, network, and MCP conformance checks

Branch: `policy-conformance-examples`
GitHub base: `main`
Logical base: `main` after #80056 merged
Status: ready for maintainer review

## Summary

This PR extends the bundled Policy plugin with three additional config-level conformance areas: model providers and model refs, private-network SSRF settings, and OpenClaw-managed MCP servers.

Policy stays a read-only conformance layer over existing OpenClaw config:

- `policy.jsonc` defines authored requirements.
- OpenClaw config remains the source of truth for observed workspace state.
- Policy extracts evidence from existing settings.
- The Policy plugin registers doctor health checks for concrete authored rules.
- `doctor --lint` and `policy check` report drift without mutating config or runtime state.
- `policy check --json` emits the recordable audit tuple: `policyHash + evidenceHash + findingsHash + clean result -> attestationHash`.

This follows the channels and tools policy slices, but keeps the added areas read-only. It proves the shape works across several OpenClaw settings categories without adding runtime enforcement or duplicate registries.

## What Changed

- Added model-provider evidence to `policy check --json`: `modelProviders` and `modelRefs`.
- Added `models.providers.allow` and `models.providers.deny` policy support.
- Added `policy/models-denied-provider` and `policy/models-unapproved-provider`.
- Added network evidence to `policy check --json`.
- Added `network.privateNetwork.allow`.
- Added `policy/network-private-access-enabled`.
- Added MCP server evidence to `policy check --json`.
- Added `mcp.servers.allow` and `mcp.servers.deny` policy support.
- Added `policy/mcp-denied-server` and `policy/mcp-unapproved-server`.
- Updated policy CLI and plugin reference docs.
- Added tests for denied rules, allowlists, evidence extraction, and empty category namespaces.

## Policy Shape

```jsonc
{
  "models": {
    "providers": {
      "allow": ["openai", "anthropic"],
      "deny": ["openrouter"],
    },
  },
  "network": {
    "privateNetwork": {
      "allow": false,
    },
  },
  "mcp": {
    "servers": {
      "allow": ["docs"],
      "deny": ["untrusted"],
    },
  },
}
```

Category blocks are namespaces. A block such as `models: {}`, `network: {}`, or `mcp: {}` does not run a check by itself. A check runs when a concrete authored requirement is present, such as `models.providers.allow`, `network.privateNetwork.allow`, or `mcp.servers.deny`.

Deny wins over allow. If a provider or server is denied, the finding reports the deny rule and does not also report the allowlist rule for the same target.

## Evidence Shape

```json
{
  "evidence": {
    "modelProviders": [
      {
        "id": "openai",
        "source": "oc://openclaw.config/models/providers/openai"
      }
    ],
    "modelRefs": [
      {
        "ref": "openai/gpt-5.5",
        "provider": "openai",
        "model": "gpt-5.5",
        "source": "oc://openclaw.config/agents/defaults/model"
      }
    ],
    "network": [
      {
        "id": "browser-private-network",
        "source": "oc://openclaw.config/browser/ssrfPolicy/dangerouslyAllowPrivateNetwork",
        "value": false
      }
    ],
    "mcpServers": [
      {
        "id": "docs",
        "transport": "stdio",
        "source": "oc://openclaw.config/mcp/servers/docs",
        "command": "npx"
      }
    ]
  }
}
```

## Safety

`doctor --lint` and `policy check` are read-only for these areas. This PR does not:

- change model config or intercept model runtime calls;
- change browser or web-fetch network config;
- edit `mcp.servers`, start MCP runtimes, stop MCP runtimes, or contact remote MCP servers;
- add gateway protocol, approval, before-tool-call, or runtime policy enforcement surfaces.

Findings identify both sides of the decision:

- `target` points to the observed OpenClaw setting.
- `requirement` points to the authored policy rule.

## Real Behavior Proof

Behavior addressed: Enabled bundled Policy registers model-provider, MCP-server, and private-network health checks, collects config-derived evidence, and emits structured findings with `oc://` target/requirement paths when configured model providers, MCP servers, or private-network SSRF posture violates policy.

Real environment tested: Windows source worktree restacked on `origin/main` after #80056 merge, head `6f6e7c8283`; previous before/after WSL proof artifact remains linked below.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:

- `pnpm docs:list`
- `node --no-maglev node_modules\\vitest\\vitest.mjs extensions/policy/src/policy-state.test.ts extensions/policy/src/cli.test.ts extensions/policy/src/doctor/register.test.ts src/flows/bundled-health-checks.test.ts --run`
- `node node_modules\\@typescript\\native-preview\\bin\\tsgo.js --project tsconfig.json --noEmit`
- `pnpm docs:check-mdx -- docs/cli/policy.md docs/plugins/reference/policy.md`
- `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD`

Evidence after fix:

![Fresh before/after proof for model, MCP, and network policy checks](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/giodl73-repo/openclaw/proof-artifacts/pr-80783-fresh/pr-80783/pr-80783-policy-model-mcp-network-conformance-before-after-proof.png)

Observed result after fix: Focused policy and bundled health-check tests passed 4 files and 70 tests. `tsgo`, docs MDX, and diff whitespace checks passed. The restacked diff is limited to Policy docs, Policy source/tests, and the bundled health-check registration test.

What was not tested: Runtime policy enforcement, approval metadata propagation, approval UI, supervisor protocol, gateway protocol, Swift protocol surfaces, and MCP runtime execution are intentionally not part of this PR. Local `oxfmt` could not be run in this Windows worktree because the formatter process failed with `spawn EPERM` / `Access is denied`; docs MDX and whitespace checks passed.

## Related

- Doctor health contract and lint validation: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/80055
- Channels-first policy conformance: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/80407
- Tool metadata conformance: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/80056
- Runtime audit metadata and attestation enforcement: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/81104

<!-- policy-stack-links:start -->

## Policy stack links

This PR is part of the Policy 1.0 proof stack:

1. #80407 — Policy: add conformance system with channel checks
2. #80056 — Policy: add tool metadata conformance
3. #80783 — Policy: add model, network, and MCP conformance checks
4. #81104 — Policy: add runtime audit metadata and attestation enforcement
5. #81974 — Policy: add secrets auth provenance checks
6. #81981 — Policy: add gateway exposure checks

<!-- policy-stack-links:end -->

target.title: fix(ios): prevent CheckedContinuation double-resume crash in WebSocket ping
target.body:
## Summary

`WebSocketTaskBox.sendPing()` crashes with `EXC_BREAKPOINT` due to a `CheckedContinuation` being resumed twice.

## Root Cause

`URLSessionWebSocketTask.sendPing` can invoke the pong handler more than once when the task is cancelled mid-flight (observed on iOS 18+). Since `CheckedContinuation` traps on double-resume, this causes an immediate crash.

Typical scenario: app backgrounds while a ping is in-flight → task cancellation races with the pong response → handler fires twice → crash.

## Fix

Wraps the continuation in a thread-safe `OneShotContinuation` (NSLock-guarded) that only allows the first resume and silently drops duplicates.

## Crash Log (redacted)

```
Exception Type: EXC_BREAKPOINT (SIGTRAP)
Exception Codes: 0x0000000000000001, 0x00000001a42dd1f0

Thread 4 Crashed:
0   libswift_Concurrency.dylib  CheckedContinuation.resume(returning:)
1   OpenClawKit                 WebSocketTaskBox.sendPing()
```

target.title: fix(security): normalize Cyrillic and Greek confusable chars in boundary marker sanitization
target.body:
## Problem

The external content marker sanitization in `foldMarkerText` normalizes fullwidth ASCII characters and Unicode angle bracket homoglyphs before checking for boundary marker spoofs. However, it does not account for **Cyrillic and Greek confusable characters** that are visually identical to their Latin counterparts.

For example, Cyrillic `А` (U+0410), `С` (U+0421), `Е` (U+0415), `О` (U+041E), `Т` (U+0422), and `Х` (U+0425) are pixel-perfect matches for Latin A, C, E, O, T, and X in most fonts. An attacker could construct markers like `<<<ЕXТЕRNАL_UNТRUSТЕD_СОNТЕNТ>>>` (with Cyrillic letters substituted) that bypass the regex check while still being interpreted as boundary markers by downstream LLMs.

## Fix

- Add a `CONFUSABLE_LETTER_MAP` covering the most common Cyrillic uppercase/lowercase and Greek uppercase/lowercase confusable characters
- Apply confusable normalization in `foldMarkerChar` before the angle bracket check
- Extend the `foldMarkerText` regex character class to match these additional code points
- Add test cases verifying both Cyrillic and Greek confusable marker bypass attempts are caught

## Testing

- All 47 existing + 2 new tests pass in `external-content.test.ts`
- No changes to public API surface; purely additive to the internal normalization logic

target.title: [Bug] cron remove: fix silent failure when job does not exist
target.body:
## Summary

The `cron remove` command (and `openclaw cron rm` / `openclaw cron delete` aliases) sometimes reports success but the job keeps running. This happens because the underlying `remove()` operation always returned `ok: true`, even when the job did not exist.

## Root Cause

In `src/cron/service/ops.ts`, the `remove()` function:

1. Always returned `{ ok: true, removed }` regardless of whether a job was found
2. Called `armTimer()` even when nothing was removed
3. Emitted a removed event only when `removed === true` — masking the problem from observability

The CLI layer printed the success message based solely on `ok: true` without checking `removed`.

## Fix

Updated `remove()` to return a richer result:

- `{ ok: false, removed: false, reason: 'store unavailable' }` — store not loaded
- `{ ok: false, removed: false, reason: 'job not found' }` — job did not exist
- `{ ok: true, removed: true, jobName: '...' }` — successfully removed

Also removed the spurious `armTimer()` call when nothing was removed.

## Verification

The `cron rm <id>`, `cron remove <id>`, and `cron delete <id>` CLI aliases all route through the same `cron.remove` gateway RPC call, so fixing the service layer fixes all three.

Fixes #28715

target.title: feat: handle location.update node event in gateway
target.body:
## Summary

- **Problem:** `location.update` events sent from iOS nodes via `node.event` were silently dropped by the gateway (`default` case in `handleNodeEvent`)
- **Why it matters:** The agent could not react to location changes (e.g. severance hooks, memory logging) because the event never reached the heartbeat prompt
- **What changed:** Added `location.update` handler in gateway that enqueues a system event and wakes the heartbeat; classified `location-update` as `wake` reason; added tagged-event detection (`location:*`) so pending location events are included in the heartbeat prompt; added logging on both iOS and gateway sides
- **What did NOT change (scope boundary):** No changes to `location.get` command flow, node command policy, iOS location permissions model, or heartbeat prompt templates

## Change Type (select all)

- [ ] Bug fix
- [x] Feature
- [ ] Refactor
- [x] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [x] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

None

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

- `location.update` events from iOS nodes now trigger a heartbeat wake and appear in the agent heartbeat prompt as a system event
- Gateway logs `location.update received node=<id> source=<source>` at info level when an event arrives

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? `No`
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? `No`
- New/changed network calls? `No`
- Command/tool execution surface changed? `No`
- Data access scope changed? `No`

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: macOS (gateway), iOS simulator
- Runtime/container: Node 25, `pnpm gateway:watch`
- Model/provider: Claude Opus 4.6
- Integration/channel (if any): iOS node
- Relevant config (redacted): `locationMode: .always`, `authorizedAlways`

### Steps

1. Start the gateway with `pnpm gateway:watch`
2. Add the following to the gateway's `HEARTBEAT.md`:
   ```
   ## Location Tracking

   - On `location.update` event, append to the Location Log table in `memory/YYYY-MM-DD.md`
   - Format: `| HH:MM | lat, lng | notes |`
   - Create the Location Log table if it does not exist
   ```
3. Open the iOS app on a real device with Always location permission granted
4. Move between locations to trigger iOS Significant Location Change events (or use Xcode > Debug > Simulate Location)

### Expected

- Xcode log shows `location.update sent to gateway`
- Gateway log shows `[gateway] location.update received node=... source=ios-significant-location`
- `memory/YYYY-MM-DD.md` is updated with a Location Log table:
  ```
  ## Location Log

  | Time (JST) | Lat, Lng | Notes |
  |---|---|---|
  | 13:41 | 37.4451, -122.2707 | Near Palo Alto, ±5m |
  | 14:03 | 37.7501, -122.4033 | San Francisco, ±5m |
  | 14:04 | 37.7575, -122.4039 | San Francisco, ±5m |
  ```

### Actual

Same as expected.

## Evidence

- [ ] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [x] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

## Human Verification (required)

- Verified scenarios: iOS simulator sends `location.update`, `sendEvent` completes without error, gateway `dist/` contains handler code after build
- Edge cases checked: missing `lat`/`lon` (early return), missing `accuracyMeters`/`source` (optional), consecutive duplicate events (deduplicated by `enqueueSystemEvent`)
- What you did **not** verify: end-to-end on real device (pending), heartbeat prompt content with location data

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [ ] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? `Yes`
- Config/env changes? `No`
- Migration needed? `No`

## Failure Recovery (if this breaks)

- How to disable/revert this change quickly: revert the 3 commits on `feature/location-update`; the `default` case resumes silently dropping the event
- Files/config to restore: `src/gateway/server-node-events.ts`, `src/infra/heartbeat-reason.ts`, `src/infra/heartbeat-runner.ts`
- Known bad symptoms reviewers should watch for: unexpected heartbeat wakes when no location events are expected; excessive system events if a node sends rapid location updates (mitigated by `enqueueSystemEvent` dedup)

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: Rapid location updates from a node could flood the system event queue and trigger frequent heartbeat wakes
  - Mitigation: `enqueueSystemEvent` deduplicates consecutive identical events per `contextKey` (`location:<nodeId>`), and the heartbeat coalesce window (250ms) batches rapid wakes

target.title: fix: retry same auth profile on transient overloaded errors before failover
target.body:
## Summary

- **Problem:** When the LLM API returns a transient `overloaded` error (HTTP 529 / `overloaded_error`), OpenClaw tries to rotate to the next auth profile. If only one profile is configured for the provider, the error is surfaced immediately with "The AI service is temporarily overloaded. Please try again in a moment." — no retry.
- **Why it matters:** During Anthropic capacity spikes (frequent in March 2026), agents with a single API key become unresponsive. Users must manually resend messages. The cron subsystem already has configurable retry (`cron.retry`), but the main LLM request path does not.
- **What changed:** Added a same-profile retry loop (up to 3 attempts, exponential backoff 2s→4s→8s, jitter 25%, capped at 30s) for `overloaded` errors when no other profile is available. Applies to both prompt-side and assistant-side error paths.
- **What did NOT change (scope boundary):** Existing profile rotation, fallback model logic, and backoff-before-failover behavior are untouched. Rate-limit, auth, billing, and format errors are NOT retried. The retry counter resets on success. No new config surface (hardcoded constants — a follow-up could expose these via `agents.defaults.llmRetry`).

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #49376
- Closes #48913
- Related #49696
- Related #24321

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

- When a single-profile provider returns `overloaded` (529), the agent now silently retries up to 3 times with exponential backoff (2s, 4s, 8s) before surfacing the error.
- Log messages at `warn` level: `overloaded — retrying same profile for <provider>/<model>: attempt=N/3 delayMs=X`.
- Failover observation logs a new decision value: `retry_same_profile`.
- If all 3 retries are exhausted, behavior is identical to before (fallback model or surface error).

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? `No`
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? `No`
- New/changed network calls? `No` (same LLM API call, just retried)
- Command/tool execution surface changed? `No`
- Data access scope changed? `No`

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: macOS (Darwin arm64)
- Runtime: Node 24
- Model/provider: Anthropic Claude Opus 4.6 (single API key profile)
- Channel: Telegram

### Steps

1. Configure an agent with a single Anthropic auth profile (no fallbacks).
2. Send a message when Anthropic API is overloaded (returns 529).
3. **Before fix:** Error surfaced immediately.
4. **After fix:** Agent retries up to 3 times with backoff, then surfaces error only if still overloaded.

### Expected

Agent retries transparently and recovers when the API comes back within ~15s.

### Actual

Agent retries (visible in warn logs) and succeeds on retry, or surfaces error after 3 failed attempts.

## Evidence

- `tsgo` type check: 0 errors ✅
- `oxlint` on changed files: 0 warnings, 0 errors ✅
- `vitest run` on `failover-observation.test.ts`: 2/2 passed ✅
- `vitest run` on `runs.test.ts`: 6/6 passed ✅

## Human Verification (required)

- Verified: TypeScript compiles, lint passes, existing tests pass.
- Verified: Code review of both prompt-side and assistant-side paths — retry only triggers for `overloaded` reason, counter resets on success and on profile rotation.
- Edge cases checked: abort signal interrupts retry sleep; counter does not interfere with existing `overloadFailoverAttempts`; `markAuthProfileGood` is called before retry to clear cooldown state.
- What I did **not** verify: Live 529 error from Anthropic (cannot reproduce on demand).

## Review Conversations

- [x] N/A (new PR)

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? `Yes`
- Config/env changes? `No`
- Migration needed? `No`

## Failure Recovery (if this breaks)

- Revert this single commit to restore previous behavior (immediate surface on overloaded).
- The retry is bounded (3 attempts, ~15s max wall-clock) and respects abort signals, so it cannot cause infinite loops.
- Watch for: warn-level log spam if overload is persistent (3 log lines per failed request vs 0 before).

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: Retry adds up to ~15s latency before surfacing a persistent overload error.
  - Mitigation: 3 retries with 2s/4s/8s backoff is a reasonable tradeoff. Users previously had to manually retry anyway. A future config option could let users tune this.
- Risk: `markAuthProfileGood` call before retry could mask a genuinely bad profile.
  - Mitigation: Only called for `overloaded` reason (transient), not for auth/billing/format errors. The profile was working before the overload.

target.title: feat(device-pairing): add structured error objects instead of null for scope escalation
target.body:
## Summary

This PR modifies the device pairing token system to return structured error objects instead of `null` or legacy error formats when scope escalation is detected or other error conditions occur. This provides better error information and type safety throughout the codebase.

## Changes Made

### Core Infrastructure (`src/infra/device-pairing.ts`)

**Type System Updates:**
- Extended `RotateDeviceTokenResult` type to include error object variant:
  ```typescript
  | {
      error: "SCOPE_ESCALATION_DETECTED";
      message: string;
      details: { role: string; requestedScopes: string[]; approvedScopes: string[] | null };
    }
  ```

**Function Changes:**
- **`ensureDeviceToken()`**: Changed return type from `DeviceAuthToken | null` to `DeviceAuthToken | { error: string; message: string; details: Record<string, unknown> }`
  - Returns `DEVICE_OR_ROLE_NOT_FOUND` error when device or role is missing
  - Returns `SCOPE_ESCALATION_DETECTED` error when requested scopes exceed approved baseline
  - Returns `DeviceAuthToken` on success

- **`rotateDeviceToken()`**: Now returns structured error object for scope escalation:
  - Previously: `{ ok: false, reason: "scope-outside-approved-baseline" }`
  - Now: `{ error: "SCOPE_ESCALATION_DETECTED", message: "...", details: {...} }`

- **`revokeDeviceToken()`**: Changed return type to return error objects instead of `null`:
  - Returns `DEVICE_NOT_FOUND` when device is missing
  - Returns `ROLE_NOT_FOUND` when role is invalid
  - Returns `TOKEN_NOT_FOUND` when token is missing
  - Returns `DeviceAuthToken` on success

### Gateway Server Methods (`src/gateway/server-methods/devices.ts`)

- Updated `deviceHandlers.rotateToken()` to handle error objects from `rotateDeviceToken()`
- Updated `deviceHandlers.revokeToken()` to handle error objects from `revokeDeviceToken()`
- Enhanced `logDeviceTokenRotationDenied()` to accept broader error types including detailed error objects
- Added error object handling with proper logging of scope escalation details

### WebSocket Message Handler (`src/gateway/server/ws-connection/message-handler.ts`)

- Updated device token retrieval to handle error objects from `ensureDeviceToken()`
- Added detailed logging for scope escalation detection with context (connId, deviceId, role, scopes, error details)
- Properly rejects handshake when scope escalation is detected

### Test Updates

All test files updated to handle the new error structure:

- `src/infra/device-pairing.test.ts`: Updated `requireRotatedEntry()` helper, rotation tests, and scope escalation tests
- `src/gateway/server.auth.compat-baseline.test.ts`: Added error handling for rotation results
- `src/gateway/server.device-pair-approve-authz.test.ts`: Added error handling for rotation results
- `src/gateway/server.device-token-rotate-authz.test.ts`: Added error handling for rotation results

## Error Object Structure

When scope escalation is detected, the following error object is returned:

```typescript
{
  error: "SCOPE_ESCALATION_DETECTED",
  message: "Requested scopes exceed approved baseline",
  details: {
    role: string,
    requestedScopes: string[],
    approvedScopes: string[] | null
  }
}
```

## Benefits

1. **Better Error Information**: Clients now receive detailed error information including the specific scope escalation reason and context
2. **Type Safety**: TypeScript now properly enforces error handling for all possible return types
3. **Consistent Error Pattern**: Follows existing error patterns in the codebase
4. **Improved Debugging**: Error details provide context for troubleshooting scope-related issues

## Debugging Improvements

### Before (returned `null`)
```
[DEBUG] Device token rotation denied
  Device ID: device-12345
  Role: operator
  Reason: scope-outside-approved-baseline
```

### After (returns detailed error object)
```
[DEBUG] Device token rotation denied
  Device ID: device-12345
  Role: operator
  Requested Scopes: ["operator.admin", "operator.write"]
  Approved Scopes: ["operator.read"]
  Error: SCOPE_ESCALATION_DETECTED
  Message: Requested scopes exceed approved baseline
  Details: {
    "role": "operator",
    "requestedScopes": ["operator.admin", "operator.write"],
    "approvedScopes": ["operator.read"]
  }
```

## Testing

- ✅ TypeScript type checking passes
- ✅ Project builds successfully
- ✅ All existing tests updated to handle new error structure
- ✅ Error handling verified in gateway message handler

## Migration Guide

This is a **breaking change** for any code that directly calls `ensureDeviceToken()` and expects a `null` return value. Callers should be updated to check for error objects:

```typescript
// Before
const token = await ensureDeviceToken(params);
if (token === null) {
  // Handle error case
  return;
}

// After
const result = await ensureDeviceToken(params);
if ("error" in result) {
  // Handle error case
  console.error(result.message);
  return;
}
// Continue with successful DeviceAuthToken result
```

## Related Issues

- Fixes scope escalation detection and reporting
- Improves error handling consistency across the device pairing system

## Additional Notes

- All changes maintain backward compatibility for successful token operations
- Error responses in the gateway are properly formatted using existing `errorShape()` utility

target.title: Add configurable Gemini safety settings for Google provider
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: Gemini safety thresholds were not configurable from `openclaw.json`, and non-`google` provider keys using the `google-generative-ai` adapter could silently ignore configured safety overrides.
- Why it matters: users need a supported way to relax or tune Gemini safety filtering, especially when they route Gemini traffic through custom provider keys or proxies.
- What changed: added `models.providers.*.safetySettings`, inject the configured thresholds into outgoing `google-generative-ai` payloads without overriding explicit payload-level values, and resolve the settings from the active provider entry via normalized provider matching.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): this does not add unsupported Gemini safety categories, does not affect `google-gemini-cli`, and does not change unrelated extension/runtime-import failures currently present on `main`.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [x] Feature
- [ ] Refactor
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [x] Integrations
- [x] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #27186
- Related #12008

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

- Users can now set Gemini safety thresholds under `models.providers.<provider>.safetySettings`.
- The setting applies to provider entries using the `google-generative-ai` adapter, not just the literal `google` key.
- Explicit request payload `config.safetySettings` still wins over config-level defaults.

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? `No`
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? `No`
- New/changed network calls? `No`
- Command/tool execution surface changed? `No`
- Data access scope changed? `No`
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation:

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: macOS
- Runtime/container: Node 24 / pnpm
- Model/provider: Gemini via `google-generative-ai`
- Integration/channel (if any): N/A
- Relevant config (redacted): `models.providers.<provider>.safetySettings`

### Steps

1. Add `safetySettings` to a provider entry using the `google-generative-ai` adapter.
2. Run the config validation and runner tests.
3. Send a Gemini payload with and without explicit `config.safetySettings`.

### Expected

- Config accepts supported Gemini thresholds.
- Invalid threshold values are rejected.
- Runtime injects config-level `safetySettings` for the active provider entry and preserves explicit payload-level values.

### Actual

- Verified locally with targeted tests and payload coverage.

## Evidence

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [ ] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

## Human Verification (required)

- Verified scenarios: `pnpm test -- src/config/config-misc.test.ts -t "safetySettings"`; `pnpm test -- src/agents/pi-embedded-runner-extraparams.test.ts -t "safety settings"`; changed-file diagnostics
- Edge cases checked: non-`google` provider key using `google-generative-ai`; explicit payload `config.safetySettings` precedence; `google-gemini-cli` non-impact
- What you did **not** verify: full `pnpm build` still fails on unrelated `main`-branch unresolved imports in `extensions/google/media-understanding-provider.ts` and `extensions/acpx/src/runtime-internals/process.ts`

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? `Yes`
- Config/env changes? `Yes`
- Migration needed? `No`
- If yes, exact upgrade steps:

## Failure Recovery (if this breaks)

- How to disable/revert this change quickly: remove `models.providers.<provider>.safetySettings` or revert commits `b0d98226f6`, `c0e53d4b61`, and `87215ae344`
- Files/config to restore: `src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/extra-params.ts`, `src/config/types.models.ts`, `src/config/zod-schema.core.ts`, related tests
- Known bad symptoms reviewers should watch for: configured Gemini safety settings not appearing on outbound payloads, or non-Gemini adapters unexpectedly receiving Google-style payload fields

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: a provider that advertises `google-generative-ai` compatibility but rejects Gemini `safetySettings` could still fail downstream.
  - Mitigation: runtime only injects on the `google-generative-ai` request path, and explicit per-request payload settings remain untouched.

target.title: feat(gateway): include usage/cost metadata in agent.wait terminal response
target.body:
## Summary

- Add optional `meta` field to `AgentWaitTerminalSnapshot` carrying per-run usage/cost metadata (token counts, last-call usage, estimated USD cost, provider, model)
- Extract usage from `EmbeddedPiAgentMeta` at run completion and estimate cost via existing `resolveModelCostConfig`/`estimateUsageCost` utilities
- Forward `meta` through both `agent.wait` response paths (cached snapshot and race-resolved snapshot)

Closes #49494
Supersedes #49499 (closed due to fork visibility change)

## Motivation

External orchestrators like Paperclip need per-run cost visibility to implement budget controls, usage dashboards, and cost attribution. Today they must parse JSONL transcripts after the fact. This change surfaces the data directly in the `agent.wait` WebSocket response, which is the natural integration point for synchronous orchestration flows.

Paperclip's adapter (`@paperclipai/adapter-openclaw-gateway`) already has `parseUsage()` ready to consume `usage`, `costUsd`, `provider`, and `model` from response metadata — it just gets `undefined` today. See Paperclip PR [#884](https://github.com/paperclipai/paperclip/pull/884).

## Design

The `meta` field is fully optional and backward-compatible — it is omitted when no usage data is available (e.g., error runs that never reached the LLM).

```typescript
meta?: {
  usage?: { input: number; output: number; cacheRead?: number; cacheWrite?: number };
  lastCallUsage?: { input: number; output: number };
  costUsd?: number;
  provider?: string;
  model?: string;
};
```

## Files changed

- `src/gateway/server-methods/agent-wait-dedupe.ts` — New `AgentWaitUsageMeta` type, extended snapshot type, `extractUsageMeta()` parser
- `src/gateway/server-methods/agent.ts` — `buildUsageMetaFromRunResult()` helper, meta injection in dispatch + both wait response paths

## Security

This change does not alter any trust boundary. The `meta` field is only included in `agent.wait` responses to authenticated gateway callers (trusted operators per the security policy). No secrets, credentials, or sensitive data are exposed — only token counts and estimated cost.

## Test plan

- [ ] Existing `agent-wait-dedupe.test.ts` tests pass (backward-compatible)
- [ ] `meta` appears in `agent.wait` response when a run completes with usage data
- [ ] `meta` is omitted when no agent meta is available (error paths)
- [ ] `costUsd` is populated when model cost config exists, omitted otherwise

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

target.title: Android: lower minSdk to 26
target.body:
## Summary

Describe the problem and fix in 2–5 bullets:

- Problem: Current minSdk version was too high, preventing users on older Android devices (API 26-30) from installing the app. Additionally, the UI relied on dynamic color features (Material You) available only in newer Android versions, which would cause crashes or rendering issues on older APIs.
- Why it matters: Lowering the minSdk to 26 (Android 8.0) significantly expands our potential user base while maintaining support for essential modern background limits and security features.
- What changed:
  - Updated build.gradle to set minSdk 26.
  - Added theme color fallbacks for devices that do not support Dynamic Color (API < 31).
  - Refactored NodeForegroundService.kt to include NotificationChannel setup and compliant startForeground logic for API 26+.
  - Added a conditional check for DnsResolver to prevent crashes on Android 8.0-10, with an automatic fallback to a direct UDP resolver (port 53).
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): No changes were made to the core business logic or higher-level API features (API 31+); those users will still experience dynamic coloring.

## Change Type (select all)

- [ ] Bug fix
- [x] Feature
- [ ] Refactor
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [x] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #
- Related #

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

- Users on Android 8.0 (Oreo) through Android 11 can now install and run the app.
- On Android 12+, the app continues to use dynamic system colors.
- On Android 8.0-11, the app will use a consistent, predefined brand color palette.

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`No`)
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`No`)
- New/changed network calls? (`No`)
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`No`)
- Data access scope changed? (`No`)

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: Android 8.0 (API 26) Emulator / Android 14 (API 34) Physical Device
- Runtime/container: Android
- Model/provider: Pixel 2 (Emulator), Pixel 7 (Physical)
- Integration/channel (if any):
- Relevant config (redacted):

### Steps

1. Build and install the app on an Android 8.0 (API 26) device/emulator.
2. Start the Node service and verify that the Foreground Notification appears correctly (validating the NotificationChannel setup).
3. Test network-related features (like gateway discovery) to ensure the DNS fallback works.

### Expected

- The app installs and opens successfully on API 26.

### Actual

- App launches without crash; theme gracefully falls back to static brand colors on API 26.

## Evidence

Attach at least one:

- [ ] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [x] Trace/log snippets [build_output.txt](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/26076480/build_output.txt)
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

## Human Verification (required)

What you personally verified (not just CI), and how:

- Verified scenarios: App launch, main navigation, and settings screen on API 26.
- Edge cases checked: Dark mode toggle on older Android versions (ensure values-night also has fallbacks).
- What you did **not** verify: Did not test on every single API level between 26 and 31 (sampled 29).

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [ ] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

If a bot review conversation is addressed by this PR, resolve that conversation yourself. Do not leave bot review conversation cleanup for maintainers.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (`Yes`)
- Config/env changes? (`No`)
- Migration needed? (`No`)

## Failure Recovery (if this breaks)

- How to disable/revert this change quickly: Revert the minSdk change in build.gradle and roll back OpenClawTheme.kt file.
- Files/config to restore: app/build.gradle, OpenClawTheme.kt.
- Known bad symptoms reviewers should watch for: UI crashes (Resource Not Found) on older devices if a fallback color is missing.

## Risks and Mitigations

None

target.title: fix(discord): preserve bound thread isolation across bot accounts
target.body:
## Summary

Describe the problem and fix in 2–5 bullets:

- Problem: In multi-bot Discord setups, a thread created for `sessions_spawn(thread=true, mode="session")` could still be picked up by another bot account's general Discord routing instead of staying attached to the active bound subagent session.
- Why it matters: This breaks thread/session isolation and can cause follow-up messages to escape the intended session scope, producing replies from the wrong bot/session context.
- What changed: Discord preflight now suppresses generic routing when another Discord account already owns an active thread binding, using the current preflight config to ignore stale or removed-account bindings.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): This does not add shared-thread routing, change same-account binding behavior, or introduce new config flags.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor
- [ ] Docs
- [x] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [ ] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [x] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #49373
- Related #

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

- Follow-up messages in a Discord thread bound to an active subagent session are no longer allowed to fall through to another bot account's general channel routing.
- Removed or stale bindings for non-running / non-configured Discord accounts no longer suppress valid follow-up messages.

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`No`)
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`No`)
- New/changed network calls? (`No`)
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`No`)
- Data access scope changed? (`No`)

- Model/provider: N/A

- Relevant config (redacted): multi-account Discord setup with thread bindings enabled for bound subagent sessions

### Steps

1. Create a Discord thread via `sessions_spawn(agentId="soren", thread=true, mode="session")` from one bot/account.
2. Ensure the thread is bound to the spawned subagent session.
3. Send a follow-up message in that same Discord thread that is also visible to another configured Discord bot/account.

### Expected

- The follow-up routes only to the active bound subagent session that owns the thread.

### Actual

- Before this change, another bot/account could miss the binding and create or use a generic Discord channel session for the same thread.

## Evidence

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [ ] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording

## Human Verification (required)

What you personally verified (not just CI), and how:

- Verified scenarios: added/ran focused regressions for cross-account thread ownership suppression, stale foreign binding ignore, root-thread-binding stale config ignore, and config-snapshot mismatch handling.
- Edge cases checked: foreign manager stopped but stale binding record remains; root Discord threadBindings enabled without active foreign account; explicit preflight config differs from runtime snapshot.
- What you did **not** verify: live Discord multi-bot end-to-end behavior in a running guild; full repo `pnpm test` is not green on this checkout due unrelated existing failures; `codex review --uncommitted` could not complete because of transport disconnects.

## Review Conversations

If a bot review conversation is addressed by this PR, resolve that conversation yourself. Do not leave bot review conversation cleanup for maintainers.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (`Yes`)

- If yes, exact upgrade steps:

## Failure Recovery (if this breaks)

- How to disable/revert this change quickly: revert this PR or temporarily disable Discord thread bindings for the affected account(s).
- Files/config to restore: `src/discord/monitor/message-handler.preflight.ts`, `src/discord/monitor/thread-bindings.manager.ts`, `src/discord/monitor/thread-bindings.ts`, `src/discord/monitor/message-handler.preflight.test.ts`
- Known bad symptoms reviewers should watch for: valid follow-up messages in bound Discord threads being dropped unexpectedly, especially in multi-account setups.

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: foreign-owner suppression could incorrectly drop messages if stale bindings are treated as active owners.
  - Mitigation: suppression ignores stale bindings for removed/non-configured accounts and uses the active preflight config instead of global runtime snapshot state.

- Risk: multi-bot/shared-thread workflows may expect non-owning bots to continue responding in the same thread.
  - Mitigation: this PR intentionally preserves exclusive ownership semantics only for active bound-session threads and does not change unbound-thread behavior.

AI-assisted. Lightly tested locally with focused Discord regression tests and manual diff review.

target.title: fix: keep tui busy during follow-up waits
target.body:
## Summary

Keep the TUI visibly busy while a run is waiting on a follow-up event after `sessions_yield`.

Before this change, the TUI could appear idle even though work was still effectively in flight. The gap showed up when the final assistant message contained the `sessions_yield` follow-up context marker: the run had ended intentionally, but the UI status dropped back to an idle-looking state.

## What changed

- add `awaiting follow-up` as a busy TUI activity state
- detect the `sessions_yield` follow-up marker in final assistant output
- preserve `awaiting follow-up` when reloading session history
- clear stale `awaiting follow-up` state back to `idle` when history no longer contains the marker
- add focused tests for both live event handling and history restore behavior

## Files

- `src/tui/tui.ts`
- `src/tui/tui-event-handlers.ts`
- `src/tui/tui-session-actions.ts`
- `src/tui/tui-event-handlers.test.ts`

## Validation

- direct smoke harness passed for:
  - final event -> `awaiting follow-up`
  - history restore -> `awaiting follow-up`

- local lint/format checks passed on the touched files

target.title: fix(discord): treat native system messages as queued system events
target.body:
## Summary

Describe the problem and fix in 2–5 bullets:

- Problem: Discord-native system messages such as channel rename/icon updates were not consistently recognized as system events, so some of them could fall through inbound preflight as ordinary user text.
- Why it matters: this lets Discord-generated metadata reach the agent as if it were user intent, which is noisy and behaviorally incorrect.
- What changed: this PR expands Discord system-message handling to cover the current native `MessageType` surface, routes those messages through queued system events, and adds regression coverage for both the mapping layer and the reported channel rename case.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): this does not change Discord routing/session logic, prompt formatting, or any non-Discord channel behavior.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [ ] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [x] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #24108
- Related #24108

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

Discord-native system messages such as channel rename/icon changes, calls, discovery warnings, invite reminders, role/app subscription notices, and similar Discord-generated events are now queued as `Discord system: ...` events during inbound preflight instead of being treated like ordinary inbound message text.

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`No`)
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`No`)
- New/changed network calls? (`No`)
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`No`)
- Data access scope changed? (`No`)
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation:

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: macOS
- Runtime/container: local repo checkout
- Model/provider: N/A
- Integration/channel (if any): Discord
- Relevant config (redacted): default Discord preflight test helpers / no special config required

### Steps

1. Receive a Discord-native system message such as `ChannelNameChange`.
2. Run inbound Discord preflight for that message.
3. Observe whether the message is dropped into `enqueueSystemEvent()` or allowed to continue as normal inbound text.

### Expected

- Discord-native system messages are converted into `Discord system: ...` text and queued via preflight.
- The reported channel rename case does not continue through the normal inbound message path.

### Actual

- Before this change, `ChannelNameChange` was not handled in the Discord system-event mapper, so the message could fall through as ordinary inbound text.

## Evidence

Attach at least one:

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [x] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

Additional notes:
- Added `extensions/discord/src/monitor/system-events.test.ts` for the audited mapping surface.
- Added a rename regression in `extensions/discord/src/monitor/message-handler.preflight.test.ts`.
- Direct local mapper verification confirmed `ChannelNameChange` now resolves to `Discord system: alice renamed the channel in Guild One #general`.

## Human Verification (required)

What you personally verified (not just CI), and how:

- Verified scenarios: direct local verification of the Discord system-event mapper for `ChannelNameChange`; static review of the preflight path confirming `resolveDiscordSystemEvent()` is the gate that enqueues and drops these messages.
- Edge cases checked: rename handling, icon-change coverage, and the broader current Discord native system-message enum surface.
- What you did **not** verify: I could not complete a clean local Vitest run in this environment because the local runner did not exit cleanly after startup, and `pnpm build` is currently blocked by an unrelated existing circular re-export in `src/plugin-sdk/synology-chat.ts`.

## Review Conversations

- [ ] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [ ] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (`Yes`)
- Config/env changes? (`No`)
- Migration needed? (`No`)
- If yes, exact upgrade steps:

## Failure Recovery (if this breaks)

- How to disable/revert this change quickly: revert this commit.
- Files/config to restore: `extensions/discord/src/monitor/system-events.ts` and the accompanying tests/changelog entry.
- Known bad symptoms reviewers should watch for: Discord-native system messages unexpectedly appearing again as ordinary inbound text, or over-broad classification of user-authored Discord message types.

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: over-classifying a Discord message type that should remain user-authored content.
  - Mitigation: keep `Default`, `Reply`, `ThreadStarterMessage`, `ChatInputCommand`, and `ContextMenuCommand` as passthroughs and cover the audited system-only cases in tests.

target.title: feat(cli): add `openclaw usage` command for token and cost summaries
target.body:
## Summary

Adds a new `openclaw usage` CLI command that displays token consumption and cost breakdowns parsed from session transcripts.

## Motivation

Multiple community requests ([#39297](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/39297), [#13219](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/13219), [#24636](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/24636)) have asked for built-in token/cost tracking. Users currently cannot answer "how much did I spend today?" or "which session is the most expensive?" from the CLI.

Previous attempts ([#8462](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/8462), [#5506](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/5506)) focused on UI dashboards and were closed. This PR takes a CLI-first approach, which aligns with OpenClaw's primary interface.

## Changes

### New file: `src/cli/usage-cli.ts` (~280 lines)

```bash
# Default: today's summary
openclaw usage

# Time ranges
openclaw usage --today
openclaw usage --week
openclaw usage --month
openclaw usage --days 14
openclaw usage --from 2026-03-01 --to 2026-03-17

# Breakdowns
openclaw usage --sessions        # Top spending sessions
openclaw usage --by-model        # Per-model comparison
openclaw usage --sessions --limit 20

# Output formats
openclaw usage --json
```

### Modified files: CLI registration (6 lines)
- `src/cli/program/subcli-descriptors.ts` — descriptor entry
- `src/cli/program/register.subclis.ts` — lazy import registration

## Implementation approach

The command leverages **existing infrastructure** that already powers the gateway usage API and session status:
- `loadCostUsageSummary()` — global totals with daily breakdown
- `loadSessionCostSummary()` — per-session model/tool/latency detail
- `discoverAllSessions()` — session file discovery

No new data collection or schema changes required.

## Evidence

TypeScript compiles cleanly (`tsc --noEmit src/cli/usage-cli.ts` — zero errors from our files). Pre-commit hooks (oxlint + oxfmt) pass.

Closes #39297
Closes #13219  
Closes #24636

target.title: test(gateway): consolidate deriveSessionTitle tests into dedicated module
target.body:
## Summary

This PR fixes an issue where heartbeat polls could overwrite explicit user-labeled session titles with transcript-derived titles like `"heartbeat"`.

## Changes

- Extracts `deriveSessionTitle` to `session-title.ts` with a dedicated test module.
- Preserves explicit session labels before transcript-derived fallbacks.
- Keeps auto-populated `origin.label` behind `firstUserMessage` so unlabeled inbound DMs can still use transcript-derived titles.
- Keeps `session-utils.ts` as a compatibility re-export site.
- Clarifies the zero timestamp test name to describe the actual `updatedAt: 0` input.

## Title precedence

1. `displayName`
2. `subject`
3. explicit `entry.label`
4. `firstUserMessage` (truncated)
5. auto-populated `entry.origin?.label`
6. `sessionId` prefix fallback

## Real behavior proof

Behavior or issue addressed: explicit session labels should survive heartbeat-style transcript summaries, while auto-populated origin labels should not override a real first user message.

Real environment tested: local OpenClaw checkout on this branch after rebasing onto current `main`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```bash
node --import tsx --eval 'import { deriveSessionTitle } from "./src/gateway/session-title.ts";
const cases = {
  explicitLabelBeatsHeartbeat: deriveSessionTitle({ sessionId: "abc123", updatedAt: Date.now(), label: "My App - Dashboard" }, "heartbeat"),
  transcriptBeatsAutoOriginLabel: deriveSessionTitle({ sessionId: "abc123", updatedAt: Date.now(), origin: { label: "Discord: Engineering" } }, "Need help with deployment"),
  originLabelFallbackWithoutTranscript: deriveSessionTitle({ sessionId: "abc123", updatedAt: Date.now(), origin: { label: "Discord: Engineering" } }, null),
  zeroUpdatedAtFallback: deriveSessionTitle({ sessionId: "abcd1234-5678-90ef-ghij-klmnopqrstuv", updatedAt: 0 }),
};
console.log(JSON.stringify(cases, null, 2));'
```

Evidence after fix:

```json
{
  "explicitLabelBeatsHeartbeat": "My App - Dashboard",
  "transcriptBeatsAutoOriginLabel": "Need help with deployment",
  "originLabelFallbackWithoutTranscript": "Discord: Engineering",
  "zeroUpdatedAtFallback": "abcd1234"
}
```

Observed result after fix: explicit labels win over heartbeat transcript text; transcript text wins over auto-populated origin labels; origin labels still act as fallback when there is no transcript; zero `updatedAt` keeps the session-id-only fallback.

What was not tested: full gateway UI rendering was not manually opened; this proof covers the extracted title derivation behavior directly.

## Validation

- `pnpm test src/gateway/session-title.test.ts src/gateway/session-utils.test.ts`
- `git diff --check upstream/main...HEAD`
