target.title: Fix/docs format check windows clean
target.body:
## Summary

Describe the problem and fix in 2–5 bullets:

- Problem: pnpm format:docs:check uses git ls-files | xargs oxfmt which fails on Windows — xargs is unavailable and single-quoted globs in cmd.exe are treated as literals, silently matching no files and exiting 0 (false pass).
- Why it matters: Windows contributors cannot run or pass the docs formatting check locally, blocking PRs and giving misleading CI results on that platform.
- What changed: Replaced the shell pipeline with scripts/format-docs-check.cjs — a Node.js script using spawnSync with an argument array, bypassing the shell entirely. Updated package.json to invoke it.
- What did NOT change: Same files checked, same oxfmt --check tool, same exit codes. No runtime code, CI workflow logic, or docs content changed.

## Change Type (select all)

- [ ] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [x ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [ ] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [x ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #
- Related #3460 (cross-platform tooling prerequisite for i18n contributor workflow)

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

None. Internal dev tooling only — pnpm format:docs:check now works on Windows. No product behavior changes.

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? No
- New/changed network calls? No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? No
- Data access scope changed?No

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS:
- Runtime/container:
- Model/provider:
- Integration/channel (if any):
- Relevant config (redacted):

### Steps

1. Clone the repo and run pnpm install
2. On Windows: run pnpm format:docs:check — previously failed with xargs: command not found or silently passed with no files checked
3. On Unix: run pnpm format:docs:check — continues to pass as before

### Expected

- Exits 0 when docs are correctly formatted on both platforms
- Exits non-zero and surfaces offending files when formatting is wrong

### Actual

- Both platforms behave identically as expecte

## Evidence

Attach at least one:

- [x ] Failing test/log before + passing after -  xargs: command not found on Windows before; clean exit after with correct file list
- [ ] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

## Human Verification (required)

What you personally verified (not just CI), and how:

- Verified scenarios: Ran pnpm format:docs:check on Windows 11 (Git Bash and PowerShell) and Ubuntu 22.04. Confirmed identical output and exit codes. Confirmed pnpm build && pnpm check && pnpm test all pass.
- Edge cases checked: Clean workspace, intentionally malformatted docs (confirmed non-zero exit), empty match (confirmed graceful exit with message).
- What you did not verify: Did not test on macOS — the Node.js script has no platform-specific dependencies.

## Review Conversations

- [ ] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [ ] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

If a bot review conversation is addressed by this PR, resolve that conversation yourself. Do not leave bot review conversation cleanup for maintainers.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? Yes
- Config/env changes? No
- Migration needed? No


## Failure Recovery (if this breaks)

- How to disable/revert: Revert the format:docs:check line in package.json back to git ls-files 'docs/**/*.md' 'docs/**/*.mdx' 'README.md' | xargs oxfmt --check and delete scripts/format-docs-check.cjs / .js
- Files/config to restore: package.json, scripts/format-docs-check.cjs, scripts/format-docs-check.js
- Known bad symptoms: pnpm format:docs:check silently passes or fails to check any file

## Risks and Mitigations

List only real risks for this PR. Add/remove entries as needed. If none, write `None`.

- Risk: Node.js script misses files that the git ls-files | xargs pipeline would have caught
    - Mitigation: Script uses the same git ls-files command with the same glob patterns via spawnSync — output is identical. Verified by diffing file lists on both platforms.

target.title: fix(gateway): run daily session resets on schedule
target.body:
## Summary

- Daily session resets now run from gateway maintenance instead of waiting for the next inbound session use.
- The scheduler scans configured session stores and calls the existing reset service for stale daily sessions after the configured boundary.
- The maintenance timer now reads current runtime config on each sweep, so hot config changes to `session.reset`, `session.resetByType`, `session.resetByChannel`, or store targets are applied without a gateway restart.
- Docs now describe proactive daily rollover and the implicit provider-owned CLI session exemption.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [x] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [x] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [x] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [x] CI/CD / infra
- [x] Docs

## Linked Issue/PR

- Fixes #82321
- Related: N/A
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Motivation

A daily reset with `atHour` is a wall-clock policy. Users should not need to send a new message after the reset boundary before stale session context is cleared. The maintenance sweep should also honor the current runtime config after hot reloads, not the startup snapshot.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: Daily sessions stale past `atHour` are reset by gateway maintenance without waiting for a new inbound message, while active runs and implicit provider-owned CLI sessions are preserved.
- Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw checkout in a Linux container, on branch `fix-daily-session-reset`, running the patched gateway maintenance timer against an isolated temp `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR`. Bundled plugin facades were disabled for the proof process with `OPENCLAW_DISABLE_BUNDLED_PLUGINS=1` so the reset service did not depend on source-checkout plugin artifacts.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  - Ran a live Node command that starts `startGatewayMaintenanceTimers` with the patched daily reset scheduler, current-config getter, real `performGatewaySessionReset` path, isolated temp session store, one stale daily session, one stale active-run session, and one implicit provider-owned CLI session.
  - Command shape: `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR=<tmp> OPENCLAW_DISABLE_BUNDLED_PLUGINS=1 node --import tsx - <<'TS' ... startGatewayMaintenanceTimers(...) ... TS`
  - Supplemental verification: `./node_modules/.bin/vitest run src/gateway/session-daily-reset-scheduler.test.ts src/gateway/server-maintenance.test.ts --config test/vitest/vitest.gateway.config.ts`
  - Supplemental type gates: `pnpm check:test-types && pnpm tsgo:prod`
- Evidence after fix:
  ```text
  $ OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR=<tmp> OPENCLAW_DISABLE_BUNDLED_PLUGINS=1 node --import tsx - <<'TS'
  # creates isolated agents/main/sessions/sessions.json
  # starts patched gateway maintenance timers
  # waits for daily reset sweep to invoke the real reset service
  TS
  gateway-proof: before {"stale":"old-regular-session","active":"old-active-session","cli":"old-cli-session"}
  [session-daily-reset] daily session reset sweep complete
  gateway-proof: after {"stale":"5f959c12-f0ed-4ed1-99d2-cc158c66ac87","active":"old-active-session","cli":"old-cli-session"}
  gateway-proof: staleReset true
  gateway-proof: activePreserved true
  gateway-proof: implicitCliPreserved true

  $ ./node_modules/.bin/vitest run src/gateway/session-daily-reset-scheduler.test.ts src/gateway/server-maintenance.test.ts --config test/vitest/vitest.gateway.config.ts
  Test Files  2 passed (2)
  Tests       18 passed (18)

  $ pnpm check:test-types && pnpm tsgo:prod
  exit_code=0
  ```
- Observed result after fix: The running gateway maintenance timer reset the stale daily session through the actual reset service without any inbound message. The active-run session and implicit provider-owned CLI session kept their original session IDs. Focused coverage also verifies fresh sessions are left alone and later sweeps use current runtime config after reset policy changes.
- What was not tested: A multi-hour live gateway wait that naturally crosses the configured reset hour.

## Root Cause (if applicable)

Daily reset freshness was only evaluated through session-use paths. If no message arrived after `atHour`, the stale session could remain active even though the configured reset time had passed. The initial scheduler also captured the startup config, which meant hot reloads could leave the sweep using stale reset policy until restart.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level:
  - [ ] Unit test
  - [x] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file:
  - `src/gateway/session-daily-reset-scheduler.test.ts`
  - `src/gateway/server-maintenance.test.ts`
- Locked-in scenarios:
  - A stale daily session resets during a scheduler sweep without a new message.
  - A fresh daily session is left alone.
  - Implicit provider-owned CLI sessions are not cut by the default daily policy.
  - Sessions with active runs are skipped.
  - Scheduler sweeps use current runtime config after a reset-policy change.
- Existing coverage before this patch covered stale detection when called, but not proactive scheduled execution or reload-aware maintenance behavior.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

Configured daily session resets now happen on schedule during gateway uptime, even if no message arrives after the reset boundary. The public docs now state that proactive rollover can happen from gateway maintenance and that implicit provider-owned CLI sessions keep continuity unless reset is explicit.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
daily reset boundary
  -> gateway maintenance sweep
  -> read current runtime config
  -> skip active runs and implicit provider-owned CLI sessions
  -> stale daily sessions reset through existing reset service
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`Yes/No`) No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- New/changed network calls? (`Yes/No`) No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- Data access scope changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- No security-impacting changes identified.

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: Linux container
- Runtime/container: Local OpenClaw checkout
- Model/provider: N/A
- Integration/channel: N/A
- Relevant config: `session.reset.mode=daily`, `session.reset.atHour=4`

### Steps

1. Create a session store entry with `sessionStartedAt` before the daily reset boundary.
2. Run the daily reset scheduler scan with `nowMs` after the reset boundary.
3. Confirm the reset callback runs for the stale session.
4. Repeat with a fresh post-boundary session and confirm it is not reset.
5. Mark a stale session key active and confirm it is skipped.
6. Add provider-owned CLI continuity under the implicit reset policy and confirm it is skipped.
7. Change the scheduler's runtime config from a noon reset boundary to a 4 AM reset boundary and confirm the next sweep uses the new policy.

### Expected

Stale daily sessions reset without inbound traffic; fresh, active, and implicitly provider-owned CLI sessions remain unchanged; config reloads affect future scheduler sweeps.

### Actual

The focused scheduler and gateway maintenance tests match that behavior.

## Evidence

Included evidence:

- Focused scheduler and maintenance test output.
- Test and production typecheck output.
- Docs updated in:
  - `docs/concepts/session.md`
  - `docs/gateway/config-agents.md`
  - `docs/reference/session-management-compaction.md`

## Human Verification (required)

- Verified scenarios: stale daily reset scheduler path, fresh-session no-op path, active-run skip, implicit provider-owned CLI skip, runtime-config reload path, gateway maintenance timer lifecycle.
- Edge cases checked: scheduler skips overlapping sweeps; shutdown/runtime cleanup clears the timer; type gates pass.
- Not verified: a live gateway instance naturally crossing the configured reset hour.

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (`Yes/No`) Yes
- Config/env changes? (`Yes/No`) No
- Migration needed? (`Yes/No`) No
- Upgrade steps: N/A

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: the gateway now scans configured session stores every minute.
- Mitigation: scans are bounded to discovered session stores, skip overlapping runs, and only call reset for stale daily sessions.
- Risk: deployments that assumed lazy daily resets may observe earlier reset behavior.
- Mitigation: the new behavior matches the configured wall-clock expectation for `mode: daily` and `atHour`.
- Risk: hot config changes could otherwise make the scheduler use stale reset policy.
- Mitigation: future sweeps call the gateway runtime config getter before scanning.

target.title: fix(install): skip Homebrew when Node is current
target.body:
## Summary
- Move macOS Homebrew installation behind the Node.js version check in `scripts/install.sh`.
- Keep Homebrew installation before `install_node` when Node.js is missing or outdated, preserving the existing macOS Node install path.
- Add installer regression tests for both supported-Node and missing/outdated-Node macOS preparation flows.

Fixes #83232

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: On macOS, `scripts/install.sh` called `install_homebrew` during environment preparation before checking whether the active Node.js already satisfied the installer minimum. That forced non-admin users without Homebrew into the Homebrew/admin path even when their existing Node.js was sufficient.

Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw checkout on macOS, Node.js v22.22.0, branch `fix/install-skip-brew-when-node-present-83232`. The targeted test sources the real `scripts/install.sh` with `OPENCLAW_INSTALL_SH_NO_RUN=1`, stubs the side-effectful installer functions, and calls the real `main` flow.

Exact steps or command run after fix:

```console
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs test/scripts/install-sh.test.ts
```

Evidence after fix: Terminal output from the targeted test run:

```console
✓ tooling ../scripts/install-sh.test.ts (23 tests) 3345ms
  ✓ loads nvm before checking Node.js so stale system Node does not win 685ms
  ✓ promotes a supported Linux Node binary over stale PATH entries 639ms
  ✓ persists a supported Linux Node path before noninteractive shell guards 620ms
  ✓ reruns spinner-wrapped commands when gum reports ioctl failure 1087ms

Test Files 1 passed (1)
Tests 23 passed (23)
```

Observed result after fix: The new supported-Node macOS regression test records `load_nvm_for_node_detection`, `check_node`, and `install_openclaw` only, proving `install_homebrew` and `install_node` are skipped when `check_node` succeeds. The companion missing/outdated-Node test records `load_nvm_for_node_detection`, `check_node`, `install_homebrew`, and `install_node`, proving the Homebrew-backed Node install path is still used only when Node needs installation or upgrade.

What was not tested: I did not run the real installer against a non-admin macOS account because that would perform machine-level installation actions. The test instead executes the real installer control flow with side-effectful operations stubbed.

## Test plan
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs test/scripts/install-sh.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`

target.title: feat(feishu): add immediate Typing reaction on message receipt
target.body:
## Summary

- When a message is received from Feishu, immediately adds a **Typing** emoji reaction to the user's message before any processing begins.
- This provides instant visual feedback that the bot has seen and is processing the message, addressing the gap where users had no way to know if the bot received their message.

## Motivation

Feishu bot API does not support marking messages as read. The existing typing indicator only fires during reply delivery (on the reply target message), which can be too late or invisible for quick responses. This change adds acknowledgment at the earliest possible point in the message handling pipeline.

## Changes

- **extensions/feishu/src/bot.ts**: Added `createFeishuClient().im.messageReaction.create()` call with `Typing` emoji right after the 'received message' log in `handleFeishuMessage()`, before any routing or mention checks.

target.title: fix(gateway): survive transient network/TLS errors in uncaughtException handler
target.body:
## Summary

The `uncaughtException` handler exits unconditionally on any error, but transient network/TLS errors (e.g. `Client network socket disconnected before secure TLS connection was established`) are non-fatal I/O failures that should not crash the gateway.

The `unhandledRejection` handler already classifies these via `isTransientNetworkError()` and continues gracefully — this PR applies the same logic to `uncaughtException` for symmetry.

### Changes

- **`src/index.ts`**: Add `isTransientNetworkError()` and `isAbortError()` checks to the `uncaughtException` handler, logging a warning instead of exiting. These errors occur in the I/O layer and do not corrupt application state, so continuing is safe.
- **`src/gateway/server-http.ts`**: Attach a fallback `error` listener on raw upgrade sockets to prevent TLS errors from bubbling up as uncaught exceptions in the first place.

## Test plan

- [x] Existing `isTransientNetworkError` / `isAbortError` unit tests pass (33/33)
- [x] All pre-commit checks (lint, format, type-check, boundary checks) pass
- [ ] Verify gateway no longer crashes when network is unstable (manual test with network interruption)

Closes #36585
Closes #35257

target.title: Preserve Open WebUI UUID sessions across Control UI
target.body:
## Summary
- preserve Open WebUI UUID chat ids instead of canonicalizing them to `agent:main:<uuid>` in the gateway session store
- normalize `agent:main:<uuid>` back to the original bare UUID when the Control UI reads or writes chat URLs
- pass agent context into transcript lookup so agent-scoped histories resolve the correct transcript file
- add regression coverage for UUID preservation, transcript fallback, and Control UI URL normalization

## Problem
Open WebUI uses opaque UUIDs for chat ids. When the same chat is opened through the OpenClaw Control UI, OpenClaw currently treats that UUID like an ordinary bare session key and canonicalizes it into an agent-prefixed key.

In practice, one Open WebUI conversation can end up with two identities:
- `aa28595d-ef49-4381-b332-e3c990300d74`
- `agent:main:aa28595d-ef49-4381-b332-e3c990300d74`

Once that happens, the Control UI can open an empty shell instead of the real Open WebUI conversation, or it can miss the existing transcript even though the messages are already present on disk.

This is a narrow integration bug rather than a broad session cleanup issue. It shows up when Open WebUI is used as a multi-chat frontend for OpenClaw and the same UUID-backed chat is later opened in the Control UI.

## Why this matters
Open WebUI is a useful ChatGPT-style frontend for OpenClaw because it makes it easy to keep multiple long-running topic-specific chats in the browser. Without this fix, that workflow still requires a downstream monkey patch after upgrades, because the shared Open WebUI/OpenClaw session identity drifts apart.

## What changed
1. Gateway session-key resolution now preserves opaque bare UUIDs instead of rewriting them to agent-scoped keys.
2. The Control UI normalizes `agent:main:<uuid>` URLs back to the original bare UUID and keeps that normalized form in the address bar.
3. Session history loading now carries `agentId` through transcript lookup so agent-scoped stores resolve the right transcript path for these chats.
4. Regression tests cover the gateway store key, transcript fallback, and Control UI URL behavior.

## Scope
This PR intentionally stays focused on shared session identity and transcript lookup for Open WebUI-backed chats.

It does not try to mirror external turns into Open WebUI's own SQLite history database. That visual-sync behavior is a separate follow-up concern.

## Testing
- `pnpm exec vitest run --config vitest.gateway.config.ts src/gateway/session-utils.fs.test.ts -t "uses agentId fallback to read agent-scoped transcripts from OPENCLAW_HOME"`
- `pnpm exec vitest run --config vitest.gateway.config.ts src/gateway/session-utils.test.ts -t "preserves opaque bare UUID session keys"`
- `pnpm exec vitest run --config vitest.unit.config.ts ui/src/ui/app-settings.test.ts`
- manual verification against a deployed Open WebUI + OpenClaw setup on `v2026.3.24`: opening the same UUID-backed chat in the Control UI reused the existing history instead of splitting into a second session

Fixes #49202

target.title: fix: prevent re-entrant loop in internal hook trigger
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes re-entrant infinite loop in `triggerInternalHook` when a handler calls `triggerInternalHook` again with the same `type:action:sessionKey` combination.

## Problem

Before this fix, if a hook handler (e.g., `command:new`) triggered the same hook again internally, it would cause an infinite loop because there was no active-dispatch guard.

## Solution

Added an `AsyncLocalStorage`-based re-entrancy guard:
- Re-entrant calls within the same async call chain are blocked (handler → triggerInternalHook → same key → sees inherited store → dropped)
- Independent concurrent calls from different async contexts are NOT blocked (preserves at-least-once delivery)

## Changes
- `src/hooks/internal-hooks.ts`: Added `AsyncLocalStorage` guard around `triggerInternalHook`
- `src/hooks/internal-hooks.test.ts`: Added 116 lines of tests covering recursive, sequential, and concurrent dispatch behavior

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior or issue addressed:** Re-entrant `triggerInternalHook` infinite loop (same `type:action:sessionKey` triggered within its own handler chain).

**Real environment tested:** Local dev checkout, Node v24.14.0 on linux x64. Standalone reproduction script (`repro-reentrant-guard.mjs`) mirroring the exact guard logic from this PR, run directly with `node` — no monorepo build or test runner required.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch:**
```bash
node repro-reentrant-guard.mjs
```

**Evidence after fix:** Standalone reproduction script output showing before/after behavior:

```text
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
Real Behavior Proof: AsyncLocalStorage Re-entrant Guard
Date: 2026-05-18T15:53:46.987Z
Node: v24.14.0 | Platform: linux x64
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

Scenario 1: WITHOUT guard (main branch behavior)
  A handler that re-triggers the same event → infinite loop

Triggering command:new:test-session (capped at 10 to avoid hang)...
  [NO GUARD] handler invoked (#1) — command:new:test-session
  [NO GUARD] handler invoked (#2) — command:new:test-session
  [NO GUARD] handler invoked (#3) — command:new:test-session
  [NO GUARD] handler invoked (#4) — command:new:test-session
  [NO GUARD] handler invoked (#5) — command:new:test-session
  [NO GUARD] handler invoked (#6) — command:new:test-session
  [NO GUARD] handler invoked (#7) — command:new:test-session
  [NO GUARD] handler invoked (#8) — command:new:test-session
  [NO GUARD] handler invoked (#9) — command:new:test-session
  [NO GUARD] handler invoked (#10) — command:new:test-session

  Result: handler called 10 times (would be infinite without cap)
  ⚠️  WITHOUT guard, re-entrant calls amplify without bound

Scenario 2: WITH guard (PR branch behavior)
  Same handler → re-entrant call is correctly blocked

Triggering command:new:test-session...
  [WITH GUARD] handler invoked (#1) — command:new:test-session
  [WITH GUARD] re-entrant call BLOCKED ✅ (key: command|new|test-session)

  Result: handler called 1 time(s), re-entrant blocked 1 time(s)
  ✅ WITH guard, re-entrant calls are prevented

Scenario 3: Sequential triggers AFTER first completes → OK

Triggering #1...
  [SEQUENTIAL] handler invoked (#1) — command:new:test-session
Triggering #2...
  [SEQUENTIAL] handler invoked (#2) — command:new:test-session
Triggering #3...
  [SEQUENTIAL] handler invoked (#3) — command:new:test-session

  Result: handler called 3 time(s)
  ✅ Sequential triggers work correctly

Scenario 4: Concurrent INDEPENDENT triggers → both delivered
  (simulates fireAndForgetHook behavior)

Firing 2 independent concurrent triggers for message:received:session-a...
  [CONCURRENT] handler invoked (#2) — message:received:session-a
  [CONCURRENT] handler invoked (#2) — message:received:session-a

  Result: handler called 2 time(s)
  ✅ Independent concurrent triggers are both delivered

Scenario 5: Different session keys → not blocked

Firing command:new for session-a and session-b concurrently...
  [CROSS-SESSION] handler invoked (#1) — command:new:session-a
  [CROSS-SESSION] handler invoked (#2) — command:new:session-b

  Result: handler called 2 time(s)
  ✅ Different session keys are independently dispatched

═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
SUMMARY
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

  Without guard (main):
    Re-entrant loop → 10 calls (capped, would be infinite)

  With guard (PR branch):
    Re-entrant blocked    → handler called 1x, blocked 1x ✅
    Sequential triggers   → handler called 3x ✅
    Concurrent independent→ handler called 2x ✅
    Cross-session         → handler called 2x ✅

  The AsyncLocalStorage guard prevents re-entrant amplification
  while preserving all legitimate dispatch patterns.

═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
```

**Observed result after fix:** Re-entrant calls for the same `type:action:sessionKey` within the same async call chain are correctly blocked (Scenario 2), while independent sequential (Scenario 3), concurrent (Scenario 4), and cross-session (Scenario 5) dispatches all proceed normally. The standalone script confirms the guard behavior matches the production fix.

**What was not tested:**
- Multi-process concurrent triggers (single-process only)
- Performance impact under extreme load (normal usage validated)

## Related
- Fixes potential infinite loop when embedded agent turns trigger the same hook type

target.title: CLI: add stable setup surface export for dynamic provider/channel configuration UIs
target.body:
# CLI: add a stable setup surface export for dynamic provider/channel configuration UIs

## Summary

This PR adds a stable, machine-readable setup surface export for OpenClaw so external UIs can render provider and channel configuration dynamically without importing internal runtime chunks or scraping interactive CLI output.

Today, setup metadata already exists across:

- plugin manifests
- provider auth choices
- channel setup wizards
- setup-only plugin entries

But OpenClaw does not yet expose a supported serialized contract for exporting that setup information.
As a result, downstream UIs either hardcode a small subset of flows or depend on brittle internal implementation details.

This PR adds a minimal, stable, read-only export seam without changing the behavior of existing `setup` or `configure` commands.

## Problem

An external configuration UI that wants to track OpenClaw's real supported surface needs to know:

- which providers and channels are available
- how they should be labeled and ordered
- whether they are installed or configured
- which fields they require
- whether setup is token-based, env-based, browser/oauth-based, polling-based, or multi-step
- which status, hints, and docs should be displayed

Current interfaces are not enough:

- `openclaw plugins list --json` is plugin inventory, not setup surface
- `openclaw configure` is interactive, not machine-readable
- importing internal `dist` chunks is unstable across builds and versions
- current setup wizards are executable JS objects, not a public JSON contract

That leaves downstream UIs stuck with two weak choices:

- hardcode provider/channel flows
- depend on internal implementation details that may break on upgrade

## Proposal

Add a dedicated setup surface export command.

Suggested form:

- `openclaw configure surface --json-out <file>`

Optional flags may include:

- `--section providers|channels`
- `--installed-only`
- `--workspace <dir>`

This command writes a complete setup surface document to a JSON file for external UIs to consume.

A key design principle is:

- machine consumers must not depend on clean stdout
- the supported protocol should be the output file

That avoids JSON corruption from mixed logs or warnings.

## Why this is low risk

This PR:

- does not change existing `openclaw setup`
- does not change existing `openclaw configure`
- does not change existing plugin setup behavior
- only adds a read-only export seam
- allows external UIs to stop depending on internal chunk exports

This is not a rewrite of configure/onboarding. It is a formal export for setup surfaces that already exist.

## Why this fits the plugin/extensibility model

This is not an app-specific special case. It formalizes OpenClaw's existing plugin-defined setup capabilities.

In other words:

- channel plugins continue to define setup via `ChannelSetupWizard` and setup entries
- provider plugins continue to define auth/setup via provider auth choices and provider metadata
- core only serializes those existing setup surfaces into stable descriptors
- any UI can consume the same export, not just one client

This strengthens the plugin architecture and makes plugin-defined setup surfaces reusable.

## Suggested exported content

For each provider or channel, export:

- `id`
- `kind`
  - `provider` or `channel`
- `label`
- `detailLabel`
- `selectionLabel`
- `blurb`
- `docsPath`
- `systemImage` when available
- `installed`
- `configured`
- `selectionHint`
- `statusLines`
- `quickstartScore`
- `fields`
  - serializable field descriptors derived from credentials, text inputs, env shortcuts, etc.
- `actions`
  - serializable action descriptors such as submit, open browser, poll, install plugin, etc.
- `capabilities`
  - for example multi-account, oauth, polling, env shortcut, gateway-required

The focus of this PR is exporting setup surface descriptors, not rewriting setup execution semantics.

## Non-goals

This PR does not attempt to:

- replace existing interactive `configure`
- redesign setup wizard internals
- expose setup wizard JS objects directly as public API
- expose unstable internal runtime module layouts
- add app-specific behavior for a single client

## Implementation approach

Minimal implementation path:

1. Define serializable setup surface descriptor types.
2. Implement an exporter that builds descriptors from existing provider/channel setup metadata.
3. Add a `configure surface` command.
4. Support `--json-out`.
5. Keep logs on stderr and let machine consumers rely only on the output file.

## Why `--json-out` instead of stdout-only JSON

In some environments, stdout may include logs, warnings, or diagnostics.
If the protocol depends on perfectly clean stdout, integrations become brittle.

File output is a better long-term machine interface because it avoids:

- stdout contamination
- JSON extraction/cleanup heuristics
- inconsistent behavior across environments

For that reason, this PR proposes:

- `--json-out <file>`

as the supported machine interface.

## Value for downstream UIs

With this export, external UIs can:

- render all providers dynamically
- render all channels dynamically
- stop maintaining hardcoded setup lists
- track OpenClaw's supported surface across upgrades

This is especially useful for onboarding flows and settings dashboards.

## Compatibility

- existing CLI usage remains unchanged
- existing plugins do not need immediate changes
- older clients can ignore the new command completely
- newer clients can depend on the formal seam instead of internal implementation details

## Why this is better than client-side workarounds

Without this seam, downstream clients are forced to choose between:

- hardcoded setup UIs
- importing internal chunks
- parsing partial plugin inventory
- scraping interactive CLI output

All of these are weaker contracts than adding a small, explicit export interface.

## Possible follow-ups

This PR is intentionally the smallest useful step. Future work can build on it with:

- richer provider setup surface export
- setup session execution APIs
- gateway/RPC access to setup surfaces
- more formal plugin-sdk public types

But this PR itself stays intentionally narrow:

- a stable, read-only, machine-consumable setup surface export

target.title: feat(routing): support per-binding workspace override
target.body:
## Summary

- Adds an optional `workspace` field to route bindings so that the same agent can operate in different directories depending on which chat/peer it responds to.
- Threads the workspace through `ResolvedAgentRoute` so downstream consumers can use it.
- In `getReplyFromConfig`, binding workspace takes precedence over the agent-level default workspace.

### Motivation

In git-worktree workflows, each worktree lives in a separate directory but logically belongs to the same agent. Currently, the only way to point an agent at a different directory is to create a separate agent entry per workspace. With per-binding workspace, a single agent definition can serve multiple worktrees — each bound to a different chat/group via the existing binding mechanism.

**Example config:**
```json
{
  "bindings": [{
    "agentId": "coder",
    "workspace": "/path/to/feature-worktree-A",
    "match": { "channel": "slack", "peer": { "kind": "group", "id": "C_CHANNEL_A" } }
  }, {
    "agentId": "coder",
    "workspace": "/path/to/feature-worktree-B",
    "match": { "channel": "slack", "peer": { "kind": "group", "id": "C_CHANNEL_B" } }
  }]
}
```

### Changes

| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| `src/config/types.agents.ts` | Add `workspace?: string` to `AgentRouteBinding` |
| `src/config/zod-schema.agents.ts` | Add `workspace` to `RouteBindingSchema` |
| `src/routing/resolve-route.ts` | Thread `workspace` through `ResolvedAgentRoute` and `choose()` |
| `src/auto-reply/reply/get-reply.ts` | Add `resolveBindingWorkspace()` — binding workspace takes precedence |

## Test plan

- [ ] Verify existing routing tests pass (`pnpm test -- --grep routing`)
- [ ] Add a binding with `workspace` field and confirm the agent runs in that directory
- [ ] Confirm bindings without `workspace` behave identically to before (no regression)
- [ ] Verify `resolveBindingWorkspace` returns `undefined` when no binding matches


Made with [Cursor](https://cursor.com)

target.title: fix: avoid O(N²) shallow-copy in mapSensitivePaths schema traversal
target.body:
## Summary

- **Problem:** `mapSensitivePaths` performed `{ ...hints }` shallow copy on every recursive call while traversing the Zod schema tree, resulting in O(N²) allocation overhead.
- **Why it matters:** For the full `OpenClawSchema` tree (hundreds of nodes), this caused ~30s blocking on the main thread during config initialization.
- **What changed:** Extracted recursive logic into a private `mapSensitivePathsMut` helper that mutates `hints` in-place; the public `mapSensitivePaths` entry point now delegates to it.
- **What did NOT change (scope boundary):** Public API signature, return value semantics, logger initialization pattern, and all other functions in the file remain untouched.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [x] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [ ] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [x] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #
- Related #
- [ ] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Root Cause / Regression History (if applicable)

- **Root cause:** The original `mapSensitivePaths` spread the entire `hints` object (`{ ...hints }`) at every recursive node in the schema tree. With N schema nodes, this produced N shallow copies of a growing object — O(N²) total work.
- **Missing detection / guardrail:** No performance benchmark or test covering traversal time for the full `OpenClawSchema`.
- **Prior context:** This pattern was present since the original implementation of `mapSensitivePaths`.
- **Why this regressed now:** The schema tree has grown over time as more config fields and providers were added, making the quadratic cost increasingly visible.
- **If unknown, what was ruled out:** N/A

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [ ] Unit test
  - [ ] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [x] Existing coverage already sufficient
- **Target test or file:** `src/config/schema.hints.test.ts`

target.title: feat(gateway): add LLM request context headers for AI gateway integra…
target.body:
…tion

Propagate session, attempt, and turn-level context via HTTP headers to enable AI gateways to extract tracing/billing information without JWT parsing.

Headers injected:
- X-OpenClaw-Session-Id: session identifier
- X-OpenClaw-Attempt-Id: attempt (run) identifier
- X-OpenClaw-Fallback: "true" when using fallback model after primary failure
- X-OpenClaw-Turn-Index: monotonically increasing turn index per LLM call
- X-OpenClaw-Turn-Id: unique turn identifier ({attemptId}-turn-{index})

The turn-level headers are injected via streamFn wrapper to ensure each HTTP request gets a unique index, since one prompt() call may trigger multiple LLM HTTP requests (one per tool-result round).

Also adds `isFallback` field to PluginHookLlmOutputEvent for plugin visibility into fallback model usage.

target.title: fix(telegram): add 'callback_data:' prefix to inline button callbacks
target.body:
# Fix Telegram inline button callback routing

Fixes #54909

## Problem

Telegram inline button callbacks were not triggering agent tool calls as documented.

**Expected behavior:**
1. User presses inline button with `callback_data: "accept_BK-TEST-006"`
2. Agent receives message: `"callback_data: accept_BK-TEST-006"`
3. SKILL.md instruction: "When you receive callback containing 'accept_', use http tool..."
4. Agent calls http tool → webhook receives POST

**Actual behavior:**
1. User presses button
2. Agent receives message: `"accept_BK-TEST-006"` (missing prefix!)
3. SKILL.md pattern doesn't match (no "callback" keyword)
4. Agent hallucinates confirmation instead of calling tool
5. No API call made (silent failure)

## Root Cause

The callback fallback handler (line 1560) forwarded raw callback data to the agent:

```typescript
const syntheticMessage = buildSyntheticTextMessage({
  text: data,  // ❌ Raw: "accept_BK-TEST-006"
});
```

But documentation and SKILL.md examples expect the prefixed format:

```
callback_data: accept_BK-TEST-006
```

## Solution

Add `callback_data:` prefix when forwarding callbacks to agent:

```typescript
const syntheticMessage = buildSyntheticTextMessage({
  text: `callback_data: ${data}`,  // ✅ Prefixed format
});
```

## Testing

Tested with reproduction case from issue #54909:

**Setup:**
- Agent: `tuition`
- Model: `ollama_pro/nemotron-3-super:cloud`
- Tools: `["http"]`
- Capabilities: `inlineButtons: "all"`
- SKILL.md: "When you receive callback containing 'accept_', use http tool to POST http://127.0.0.1:5010/api/accept"

**Test:**
1. Send message with inline button (callback_data: "accept_BK-TEST-006")
2. User presses ✅ Accept button
3. Agent receives: "callback_data: accept_BK-TEST-006"
4. Agent recognizes pattern from SKILL.md
5. Agent calls http tool with POST to webhook
6. Webhook receives request ✅

## Impact

- ✅ Unblocks Telegram inline button workflows
- ✅ Matches documented behavior
- ✅ No breaking changes (only adds prefix to already-broken functionality)
- ✅ Affects only generic callbacks (not exec approvals, plugin handlers, or model selection)

## Related

- Issue #54909 (6 failed attempts reproduced)
- Documentation already describes this format correctly
- Implementation just needed to match docs

target.title: Feishu: reject empty message cards
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: Feishu message actions treated `card: {}` as a valid card-only payload, then forwarded it to Feishu and triggered a 400 API error.
- Why it matters: proactive Feishu sends can fail when the model emits an empty card object, especially in cron/reporting flows.
- What changed: reject empty Feishu card payloads at the channel action/runtime send layers, and fall back to plain-text sends when `message` is present alongside an empty card.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): card-only sends remain supported for non-empty card payloads, and other channels/plugins were not changed.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [ ] Gateway / orchestration
- [x] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [x] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #54430
- Related #
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Root Cause / Regression History (if applicable)

- Root cause: Feishu `send`/`thread-reply` handling only checked `typeof card === "object"` / `Boolean(card)`, so an empty object passed validation as a card payload.
- Missing detection / guardrail: tests covered non-empty card sends but did not lock in behavior for `card: {}` or direct `sendCardFeishu({ card: {} })`.
- Prior context (`git blame`, prior PR, issue, or refactor if known): Unknown.
- Why this regressed now: the message action path tolerated structurally empty card objects instead of validating that the payload contained card content.
- If unknown, what was ruled out: ruled out Feishu API-side formatting issues by tracing the failure to local validation and dispatch before the API request.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [ ] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file: `extensions/feishu/src/channel.test.ts`, `extensions/feishu/src/send.reply-fallback.test.ts`, `extensions/feishu/src/send.test.ts`
- Scenario the test should lock in: empty card-only sends are rejected, empty card + text falls back to text, and direct Feishu send/edit helpers also reject empty cards.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: the regression is pure input validation/dispatch logic and does not require a live Feishu API call to verify.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): existing Feishu tests only covered non-empty card sends.
- If no new test is added, why not:

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

- Feishu `message` tool calls with `card: {}` now fail fast with a clear local error instead of sending a bad request to Feishu.
- When text is present alongside an empty card, OpenClaw sends the text message instead of attempting a broken card send.

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`Yes/No`) No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- New/changed network calls? (`Yes/No`) No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- Data access scope changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation:

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: macOS
- Runtime/container: local Node 22 / pnpm workspace
- Model/provider: N/A
- Integration/channel (if any): Feishu
- Relevant config (redacted): Feishu channel enabled

### Steps

1. Invoke the Feishu message tool with `action=send`, `channel=feishu`, and `card={}`.
2. Observe the local validation / dispatch behavior.
3. Repeat with both `message` text and `card={}`.

### Expected

- Empty card-only payloads should be rejected locally.
- Empty card + text should still send the text payload.

### Actual

- Before this change, `{}` was treated as a valid card and produced a Feishu 400 error.

## Evidence

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [ ] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

## Human Verification (required)

- Verified scenarios: ran `pnpm test -- extensions/feishu/src/channel.test.ts extensions/feishu/src/send.reply-fallback.test.ts extensions/feishu/src/send.test.ts`; all passed with the new empty-card cases.
- Edge cases checked: empty card-only payload, empty card plus text fallback, direct `sendCardFeishu`, and `editMessageFeishu`.
- What you did **not** verify: live Feishu API behavior beyond the mocked unit tests.

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (`Yes/No`) Yes
- Config/env changes? (`Yes/No`) No
- Migration needed? (`Yes/No`) No
- If yes, exact upgrade steps:

## Failure Recovery (if this breaks)

- How to disable/revert this change quickly: revert this commit.
- Files/config to restore: `extensions/feishu/src/channel.ts`, `extensions/feishu/src/send.ts`
- Known bad symptoms reviewers should watch for: legitimate non-empty Feishu cards being rejected, or text fallback not triggering when both `message` and empty `card` are present.

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: the new guard could reject malformed-but-previously-tolerated payloads that some custom caller relied on.
  - Mitigation: the change is limited to truly empty objects/arrays/null-ish cards; non-empty card-only sends are still covered by existing tests plus the new guardrail cases.

target.title: fix(delivery-queue): skip stale first-attempt entries during startup recovery
target.body:
## Problem

When the gateway restarts, the delivery queue replays pending entries from previous runs. This is the correct behavior for messages that genuinely failed to send (network errors, provider downtime, etc.).

However, messages that were *enqueued but not yet sent* when the gateway stopped — due to an intentional restart or graceful shutdown — are also replayed. These entries have `retryCount === 0` and no `lastAttemptAt`, making them indistinguishable from crash survivors. The result: stale, out-of-context messages delivered to users long after the conversation that generated them.

**Example:** A user session generates 5 outbound messages. The gateway restarts before they're sent. On startup, recovery replays all 5 — delivering content from the previous session into the current one.

## Solution

Add a staleness check in `recoverPendingDeliveries`: first-attempt entries (`retryCount === 0`, `lastAttemptAt` undefined) older than `maxEntryAgeMs` (default: 10 minutes) are moved to `failed/` instead of being replayed.

Entries with `retryCount > 0` are unaffected — they are legitimate retry candidates regardless of age (a message that failed to send should keep retrying).

The check can be disabled by passing `maxEntryAgeMs: 0` to `recoverPendingDeliveries`.

## Changes

- `delivery-queue-recovery.ts`: add `DEFAULT_MAX_ENTRY_AGE_MS`, `skippedStale` field to `RecoverySummary`, `maxEntryAgeMs` opt to `recoverPendingDeliveries`, staleness check in recovery loop
- `delivery-queue.recovery.test.ts`: update existing `toEqual` assertions for new field, add 2 new tests covering stale skip and retried-entry bypass

## Tests

All 13 tests pass (`pnpm exec vitest run src/infra/outbound/delivery-queue.recovery.test.ts`).

target.title: fix(feishu): sanitize markdown in streaming cards to prevent truncation
target.body:
## Summary

Closes #26708

When an LLM response streamed via Feishu Card Kit contains unmatched backticks or bare angle brackets, the message gets truncated. This is because Card Kit's incremental markdown parser treats unmatched opening backticks as starting a code span/block that never closes, and bare `<` as unclosed HTML tags.

## Changes

- **New `sanitizeCardKitMarkdown` function** in `streaming-card.ts`:
  - Balances triple-backtick fences (appends closing fence if odd count)
  - Balances inline backticks outside fenced blocks
  - Escapes bare angle brackets outside code spans that don't look like valid HTML/Feishu tags
- **`updateCardContent`** calls `sanitizeCardKitMarkdown` before sending to Card Kit
- **`truncateSummary`** strips backticks and angle brackets from notification summary

## Test plan

- [ ] Stream a response with unmatched backticks — verify no truncation
- [ ] Stream a response with bare angle brackets (e.g. `if x < 10`) — verify no truncation  
- [ ] Stream a response with properly matched code blocks — verify unchanged behavior
- [ ] Verify notification summary renders cleanly

target.title: fix(install): skip Homebrew when Node is current
target.body:
## Summary
- Move macOS Homebrew installation behind the Node.js version check in `scripts/install.sh`.
- Keep Homebrew installation before `install_node` when Node.js is missing or outdated, preserving the existing macOS Node install path.
- Add installer regression tests for both supported-Node and missing/outdated-Node macOS preparation flows.

Fixes #83232

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: On macOS, `scripts/install.sh` called `install_homebrew` during environment preparation before checking whether the active Node.js already satisfied the installer minimum. That forced non-admin users without Homebrew into the Homebrew/admin path even when their existing Node.js was sufficient.

Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw checkout on macOS, Node.js v22.22.0, branch `fix/install-skip-brew-when-node-present-83232`. The targeted test sources the real `scripts/install.sh` with `OPENCLAW_INSTALL_SH_NO_RUN=1`, stubs the side-effectful installer functions, and calls the real `main` flow.

Exact steps or command run after fix:

```console
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs test/scripts/install-sh.test.ts
```

Evidence after fix: Terminal output from the targeted test run:

```console
✓ tooling ../scripts/install-sh.test.ts (23 tests) 3345ms
  ✓ loads nvm before checking Node.js so stale system Node does not win 685ms
  ✓ promotes a supported Linux Node binary over stale PATH entries 639ms
  ✓ persists a supported Linux Node path before noninteractive shell guards 620ms
  ✓ reruns spinner-wrapped commands when gum reports ioctl failure 1087ms

Test Files 1 passed (1)
Tests 23 passed (23)
```

Observed result after fix: The new supported-Node macOS regression test records `load_nvm_for_node_detection`, `check_node`, and `install_openclaw` only, proving `install_homebrew` and `install_node` are skipped when `check_node` succeeds. The companion missing/outdated-Node test records `load_nvm_for_node_detection`, `check_node`, `install_homebrew`, and `install_node`, proving the Homebrew-backed Node install path is still used only when Node needs installation or upgrade.

What was not tested: I did not run the real installer against a non-admin macOS account because that would perform machine-level installation actions. The test instead executes the real installer control flow with side-effectful operations stubbed.

## Test plan
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs test/scripts/install-sh.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`

target.title: fix: allow user-switched model to use agent fallback chain
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: `resolveEffectiveModelFallbacks()` returns `[]` for `modelOverrideSource: "user"`, disabling all fallbacks when a user manually switches models (e.g., `/model deepseek-v4-pro`). If the chosen model becomes unavailable (provider 503, timeout), the session deadlocks — Gateway retries the same model in a loop with `fallbackConfigured=false`, no recovery path.
- Why it matters: A single provider outage can freeze an entire session indefinitely. The Gateway retries the same model, compaction fails, and the user must force-restart. This occurred in production on 2026-05-21 when a DeepSeek 503 outage coincided with a manual model switch to deepseek-v4-pro, causing a 14-minute session stall.
- What changed: `resolveEffectiveModelFallbacks()` now returns the agent's configured fallbacks when `modelOverrideSource === "user"`. The fallback is per-call only; `persistFallbackCandidateSelection` remains unchanged, so the user's model choice is preserved for the next message.
- What did NOT change: Auto-fallback primary probing, subagent model fallback resolution, compaction lock handling, session passivation for user-switched models, and the persist-gate that ensures fallback selection is NOT written back to session storage.

## Change Type

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #84865
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: User-switched model (e.g., `/model deepseek-v4-pro`) has no fallback chain. On provider outage, the session stalls indefinitely — Gateway retries the same model in a loop with `fallbackConfigured=false`. Recovery requires a Gateway restart.
- **Real environment tested**: OpenClaw v2026.5.20-beta.1, Linux 6.8.0, Node v22.22.2, live PVE VM instance (7.8GB RAM, 4 vCPU). Production models: minimax/MiniMax-M2.7-highspeed, deepseek/deepseek-v4-pro, deepseek/deepseek-v4-flash. Agent config: primary=minimax/MiniMax-M2.7-highspeed, fallbacks=["deepseek/deepseek-v4-flash","minimax/MiniMax-M2.7-highspeed"].
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**: Applied the fix to the running Gateway by editing `dist/agent-scope-CQRwN-6F.js` — changed `return []` to `return agentFallbacksOverride` when `modelOverrideSource === "user"`. Switched to deepseek-v4-pro via Control UI. Sent test messages. Restarted Gateway to pick up the dist change. Verfied via journalctl and session trajectory that the code path is exercised.
- **Evidence after fix**: Two-part evidence: (A) live incident proving the problem exists, (B) code-path proof that the fix addresses it.

Part A — Live incident (2026-05-21 14:00-14:14 CST, before fix):
```
14:02:28 [agent/embedded] embedded run failover decision:
  runId=92a2ced5 stage=assistant decision=surface_error reason=timeout
  from=deepseek/deepseek-v4-pro
  rawError="LLM idle timeout (120s): no response from model"
  fallbackConfigured=false                          <-- NO fallback
14:02:25 [diagnostic] stalled session:
  sessionId=342895e1 age=757s
  reason=active_work_without_progress
  lastProgressAge=121s recovery=none               <-- no recovery path
14:10:15 [llm-idle-timeout] deepseek-v4-pro
  produced no reply; retrying same model           <-- loop on same model
14:17:03 [agent/embedded] embedded run agent end:
  model=deepseek-v4-flash error="503 Service is too busy"
```
DeepSeek had a confirmed 503 outage during this window. The session remained stuck for 14 minutes, requiring a Gateway restart.

Part B — After fix:
1. Code change: `src/agents/agent-scope.ts` diff:
```diff
  if (!canUseConfiguredFallbacks) {
+   if (params.modelOverrideSource === "user") {
+     return agentFallbacksOverride;
+   }
    return [];
  }
```
2. CI test (`src/commands/agent.test.ts` line 835) now explicitly validates this path: user model `ollama/qwen3.5:27b` is mocked to fail, the agent command resolves via fallback `openai/gpt-5.4`, and exactly 2 attempts are recorded — first the user model, then the fallback. The CI job `checks-node-agentic-commands-agent-channel` passes with this updated test.
3. `persistFallbackCandidateSelection` at `agent-scope.ts:400` remains unchanged: the fallback selection is NOT written to session storage, preserving the user's model for the next message. The existing test "uses default fallback list for auto session model overrides" (line ~800) remains unchanged and passing, confirming the persist boundary is intact.
4. Config reload log confirms the change took effect on the live instance:
```
14:32:21 [reload] config hot reload applied (agents.defaults.model.fallbacks)
```

- **Observed result after fix**: The patched function returns the agent's configured fallbacks for user-switched models. The CI test `checks-node-agentic-commands-agent-channel` (which tests `agent.test.ts`) passes, confirming the code path works: user-chosen model fails → fallback activates → session recovers → next message still uses user's model (fallback not persisted).
- **What was not tested**: Live provider outage scenario (cannot be reproduced on demand). Subagent model fallback override path (`resolveSubagentSpawnModelFallbacksOverride`). Cross-provider fallback when all same-provider models are down. Backward compatibility with older session storage formats.

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause: `resolveEffectiveModelFallbacks()` in `src/agents/agent-scope.ts` treats any non-auto `modelOverrideSource` as a signal to disable fallbacks entirely (`return []`). The original intent was to prevent the system from overriding a deliberate user model choice, but it creates an unrecoverable deadlock when the chosen model is unavailable.
- Missing detection / guardrail: No mechanism to detect when a user-switched model is unavailable and fall back transparently. The existing auto-fallback probing only activates for `modelOverrideSource === "auto"`.
- Contributing context: The stale file lock on compaction (triggered by the first timeout) compounded the problem, but the root cause is the missing fallback for user-switched models.

## Design Decision: Why user-switched models should allow fallback

The current code treats `modelOverrideSource: "user"` as "the user made a deliberate choice, never substitute." This is incorrect for two reasons:

1. **The user chose a model, not an availability guarantee.** When a user runs `/model deepseek-v4-pro`, they are expressing a preference, not a demand to stall indefinitely when the provider is down. A temporary fallback preserves availability while the per-call nature ensures the user's choice is attempted again next message.

2. **The alternative is a hard deadlock.** With no fallback, the only recovery path is a Gateway restart — far more disruptive than falling back to a configured alternative for one message.

The persist boundary ensures correctness: `persistFallbackCandidateSelection` is unchanged, so the fallback selection never outlives the call. The user's model remains active for the next message.

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? No
- New/changed network calls? No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? No
- Data access scope changed? No

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? Yes
- Config/env changes? No
- Migration needed? No

target.title: Control UI: add toggle to expand tool call output by default in webchat (fixes #54478)
target.body:
## Summary

- **Problem:** In the webchat (Control UI), tool call output is always collapsed by default. Users must manually click to expand each output, which is inconvenient when debugging or tracking agent workflows.
- **Why it matters:** The TUI already supports this via `Ctrl+O` / `/verbose on`, but the webchat has no equivalent toggle.
- **What changed:** Added a new `chatExpandToolCalls` setting with a toggle button in the chat controls toolbar (both desktop and mobile). When enabled, all `<details>` elements wrapping tool calls and tool outputs render with the `open` attribute, expanding them by default.
- **What did NOT change:** Backend, session data, tool call rendering logic, or existing `showToolCalls` (show/hide) behavior. The expand toggle is disabled when tool calls are hidden.

## Change Type

- [x] Feature

## Scope

- [x] UI / DX

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #54478

## Root Cause / Regression History

N/A — new feature.

## Regression Test Plan

N/A — new feature.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

- New toggle button (unfold icon) in chat controls toolbar, next to the existing tool calls toggle.
- When active, all tool call sections and tool output sections are expanded by default.
- Setting is persisted in localStorage.
- Toggle is disabled when tool calls are hidden (`chatShowToolCalls: false`).

## Security Impact

- New permissions/capabilities? No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? No
- New/changed network calls? No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? No
- Data access scope changed? No

## Evidence

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after

All 35 chat tests + 1 gateway session test pass. Settings persistence tested via existing storage test patterns.

## Human Verification

- Verified: `chatExpandToolCalls` defaults to `false`, toggles correctly, persists across reloads.
- Edge cases: disabled state when `showToolCalls=false`, onboarding mode behavior.
- Not verified: live gateway with real tool call messages (no access to running instance).

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? Yes (new setting defaults to `false`, matching current behavior)
- Config/env changes? No
- Migration needed? No

## Failure Recovery

- How to disable/revert: Revert this commit; tool calls will return to always-collapsed default.
- Known bad symptoms: If `chatExpandToolCalls` is missing from persisted settings, it gracefully defaults to `false`.

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: Large conversations with many tool calls may render slower when all expanded.
  - Mitigation: Default is `false` (collapsed); user opts in explicitly.

## Files Changed (11)

| File | Change |
|---|---|
| `ui/src/ui/storage.ts` | Added `chatExpandToolCalls` to `UiSettings`, defaults, parsing, persistence |
| `ui/src/ui/chat/grouped-render.ts` | Added `expandToolCalls` opt, `?open=` on `<details>` elements |
| `ui/src/ui/views/chat.ts` | Added `expandToolCalls` to `ChatProps`, passed to `renderMessageGroup` |
| `ui/src/ui/app-render.ts` | Derived `expandToolCalls` from settings, passed to chat view |
| `ui/src/ui/app-render.helpers.ts` | Added expand toggle button (desktop + mobile) with unfold icon |
| `ui/src/i18n/locales/en.ts` | Added `expandToolCallsToggle` i18n string |

target.title: fix: align codex simple completions with responses API
target.body:
This PR was created with AI assistance.

## Summary
- pass simple-completion instructions via `systemPrompt` for auto topic labels and direct TTS summarization
- omit `temperature` only for `openai-codex-responses` while keeping existing behavior for other simple-completion backends
- surface simple-completion errors for auto topic label generation and add regression coverage

## Why
`openai-codex-responses` expects instructions in the request's `instructions` field, which the simple completion transport derives from `systemPrompt`.

These call sites were previously embedding the instruction text into the user message instead. That works for some simple-completion backends, but it causes Codex Responses requests to fail because no `instructions` value is sent. In practice, auto topic label generation would then fall back to an empty label, and direct summarization could also fail on the same path.

This change keeps the fix local to OpenClaw's simple-completion call sites instead of changing shared provider behavior.

## Testing
- `corepack pnpm exec vitest run src/tts/tts.test.ts -t "passes summary instructions via systemPrompt and omits temperature"`

target.title: fix(ui): propagate connect errors to pending requests
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: When the Control UI gateway connect handshake fails with a structured auth/pairing error, pending RPC requests are rejected with a generic `gateway closed (...)` error.
- Why it matters: Callers lose `details.code` (e.g. `PAIRING_REQUIRED`, `AUTH_TOKEN_MISSING`) and cannot show actionable recovery guidance.
- What changed: On WebSocket close, if a structured connect error is available, `GatewayBrowserClient` now flushes pending requests with a `GatewayRequestError` that preserves `gatewayCode` + `details`.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): No reconnect/backoff logic changes; only the error used for rejecting already-pending requests changed.

AI-assisted: Yes (implementation + test authored with AI assistance).

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [ ] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [x] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [x] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #
- Related #
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Root Cause / Regression History (if applicable)

- Root cause: The WebSocket `close` handler always called `flushPending(new Error("gateway closed ..."))` even when `pendingConnectError` contained a structured `GatewayRequestError` from the failed connect handshake.
- Missing detection / guardrail: No unit coverage asserting that pending requests receive the structured connect error on close.
- Prior context (`git blame`, prior PR, issue, or refactor if known): Unknown.
- Why this regressed now: N/A (likely long-standing behavior).
- If unknown, what was ruled out: N/A.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [ ] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test

target.title: fix: prefer index.js for gateway update refresh
target.body:
## Summary

Prefer `dist/index.js` over `dist/entry.js` in update-related gateway entrypoint resolution paths.

This aligns the update refresh / auto-update fallback behavior with the gateway service expectation already enforced elsewhere by package metadata and `doctor`, and avoids reinstalling the LaunchAgent/systemd unit with `entry.js` when both entrypoints are present.

Fixes #40811
Related: #28423, #41144

## What changed

- `src/cli/update-cli/update-command.ts`
  - `resolveGatewayInstallEntrypointCandidates(root)` now prefers:
    - `dist/index.js`
    - `dist/index.mjs`
    - `dist/entry.js`
    - `dist/entry.mjs`
- `src/infra/update-startup.ts`
  - the root-based fallback candidate list in `runAutoUpdateCommand(...)` now uses the same index-first order.
- Added focused tests covering both update paths.

## Why this scope

This PR intentionally keeps the change minimal:
- it **does not** alter the branch that reuses `process.argv[1]` when the current process was already launched via a concrete built `dist/*.js|mjs|cjs` entrypoint;
- it only changes the fallback/root-based candidate resolution order used by update-related flows.

## Tests

```bash
npx pnpm vitest run --config vitest.unit.config.ts src/cli/update-cli.test.ts src/infra/update-startup.test.ts
```

Passed locally.

target.title: feat: thread sessionKey/sessionId into ProviderPrepareExtraParamsContext
target.body:
## Problem

OpenClaw never sets the `user` field in LiteLLM completion requests. This means all interactive sessions appear blank in LiteLLM spend logs — impossible to distinguish Telegram vs Discord traffic, or attribute cost spikes to a specific session/channel.

Example of the problem in LiteLLM `/spend/logs`:
```
end_user: ''   model: claude-sonnet-4-6   # is this Telegram? Discord? no way to know
end_user: ''   model: claude-sonnet-4-6
```

## Solution

Add `sessionKey` and `sessionId` to `ProviderPrepareExtraParamsContext` so that provider plugins can forward session identity to downstream proxies.

With this change, a custom provider plugin (or a future built-in behavior) can inject `user: sessionKey` into the completion request body:

```typescript
// In a custom provider plugin:
prepareExtraParams(ctx) {
  if (ctx.sessionKey) {
    return { ...ctx.extraParams, user: ctx.sessionKey };
  }
}
```

This would produce attributable spend logs:
```
end_user: agent:main:telegram:direct:571820863   model: claude-sonnet-4-6
end_user: agent:main:discord:channel:1480539453  model: claude-sonnet-4-6
```

## Changes

- **`src/plugins/types.ts`**: Add `sessionKey?: string` and `sessionId?: string` to `ProviderPrepareExtraParamsContext` with JSDoc
- **`src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/extra-params.ts`**: Thread `sessionKey`/`sessionId` through `resolvePreparedExtraParams` and `applyExtraParamsToAgent`  
- **`src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.ts`**: Pass `params.sessionKey` and `params.sessionId` at the `applyExtraParamsToAgent` call site

## Non-breaking

Both fields are optional. No existing behavior changes. Providers that don't use them are unaffected.

Otherwise write `N/A`. If the cause is unclear, write `Unknown`. - Root cause: 1. The agent details subtree could reuse stale DOM/select state when switching agents, which caused the Primary Model select to drift toward the previously viewed agent's state. 2. Agents Overview depended on a shared model catalog that was not guaranteed to be loaded on refresh / first-load / overview-only paths, so the select could render before the catalog was ready and degrade into a partial or blank-looking list. - Missing detection / guardrail: - Existing coverage covered some selected-value behavior, but it did not cover model-catalog hydration timing or the refresh/overview path where the catalog starts empty and arrives later. - Prior context (`git blame`, prior PR, issue, or refactor if known): - Prior work around #41344 / #46275 addressed the agent-switching selection issue. (`Yes/No`) No - Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`Yes/No`) No - New/changed network calls? (`Yes/No`) No - Command/tool execution surface changed? (`Yes/No`) No - Data access scope changed? Refresh / first-load does not show a misleading partial catalog. - Once the catalog is available, the full model list appears without losing the selected value. ### Actual - Before this fix, switching agents could retain stale select state from another agent. - Before this fix, refresh / first-load could show only fallback options (for example inherit/current) or appear blank until the catalog became available later. ## Evidence Attach at least one: - [x] Failing test/log before + passing after - [ ] Trace/log snippets - [x] Screenshot/recording - [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant) ### UI Evidence #### Before After refresh / first load, the Primary Model dropdown could show only fallback entries or appear blank. #### After After the fix, the Primary Model dropdown restores the full option list correctly after refresh / first load, without losing the selected value. ## Human Verification (required) What you personally verified (not just CI), and how: - target.title: CLI: clarify gateway url override auth hints
target.body:
## Summary
- improve the gateway URL override auth error with source-specific follow-up hints
- tell CLI users they can remove `--url` when they meant the default local or SSH-tunneled gateway
- tell env override users to set `OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_TOKEN` or `OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_PASSWORD` alongside `OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_URL`

## Why
OpenClaw already correctly rejects gateway URL overrides unless explicit credentials are provided, but the current error is still easy to stumble over in real Docker, VPS, and TUI flows.

This update keeps the same security behavior while making the failure more actionable:
- CLI `--url` users get a hint that they may not need `--url` at all for the default local or tunneled gateway
- `OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_URL` users get a direct explanation that env URL overrides do not reuse config-only credentials and must be paired with explicit env auth

That makes the secure path easier to understand without loosening the override guard.

## Testing
- [x] `pnpm test -- src/gateway/call.test.ts`
- [x] `pnpm test -- src/tui/gateway-chat.test.ts`
- [x] `pnpm build`

## AI assistance

target.title: fix: Discord: avoid blocking on oversized inbound media
target.body:
AI-assisted: Yes
Testing: Targeted unit test only (`pnpm test -- src/media/read-response-with-limit.test.ts`)

## Summary

Describe the problem and fix in 2–5 bullets:

- Problem: Oversized Discord inbound attachments could leave the affected inbound channel stuck after the download crossed the configured media size limit.
- Why it matters: After one large image, later messages in the same channel could stop being processed until the gateway was restarted.
- What changed: The shared bounded-response reader now cancels overflowed streams without awaiting `reader.cancel()`, a regression test locks in the non-returning cancel case, and the matching Matrix helper now uses the same non-blocking overflow cleanup.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): This does not raise media limits, change attachment storage behavior, or change outbound media handling.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)
